# Jiucaihezi Studio

**Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.**  
**2026-07-20**

> Each section in this document is one PPT slide.  
> If it does not appear on the PPT, it does not belong in this document.

---

## 01 | Cover

**One account. Every model.**  
**A second brain that never forgets.**

Jiucaihezi Studio  
Desktop App + Web

Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.

---

## 02 | The Problem: One Model, One Product

Want Grok? Use Grok.  
Want GPT? Go to OpenAI.  
Want Claude? Switch again.  
Want China-based models? Open another set of apps and accounts.

The result:

- Too many tools
- Too many accounts
- Too many histories
- Too many disconnected assets

**The models keep getting better. The creative process keeps getting fragmented.**

---

## 03 | The Problem: Global Models Are Hard to Access from Mainland China

For users in mainland China, reliably using overseas models often means:

- A VPN or other access setup
- An overseas email address
- Identity verification
- An international credit card
- The ongoing risk of account suspension

**Huge setup costs. Too many barriers. Constant uncertainty.**

---

## 04 | The Advantage: One Account, Global Model Access

**Sign in once.**  
Access leading models from China and around the world in one system.

- No switching between apps
- No switching between accounts
- No rebuilding your setup for every model

**There can be many models. There only needs to be one entry point.**

---

## 05 | Model Matrix: Leading Models in One Place

### Large Language Models

- Claude: Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 5 / Fable 5
- GPT: GPT 5.6 — all three modes
- Grok 4.5
- Gemini 3.5 Flash
- DeepSeek Pro
- GLM 5.2

### Image and Video Models

- CDA 12.0
- Grok
- Sora 2
- GPT Image 2
- Banana Pro
- Plus leading models from China and around the world

**Leading global and Chinese models, available through one account.**

---

## 06 | The Problem: Both Existing Paths Fall Short

Most people use AI for creative work in one of two ways.

### Path A | Coding Agents

- Burn through tokens
- Still forget important details
- Built around programming, not creative production

**Powerful, heavy, expensive—and still forgetful.**

### Path B | Direct Model Chat

- Little structure or constraint
- Easy to drift off track
- Weak support for Skills, MCP, and plugins
- Less capability, with the same memory problem

**Fast, loose, limited—and still forgetful.**

**One path is heavy and forgetful. The other is loose and forgetful.**

---

## 07 | The Solution: Creative Mode

Creative Mode is not a coding agent with a new skin.  
It is not a basic chat wrapper.

It combines the best of both approaches:

- **As fast as direct model access**
- **As structured and tool-capable as a coding agent**

Creative Mode can:

- Read and write to the current project
- Call Skills
- Call MCP tools
- Use plugins and other tools
- Directly generate images, videos, and audio

**Fast, but not uncontrolled. Powerful, but not clumsy.**

---

## 08 | The Second Brain: Where the Idea Came From

The Second Brain concept is inspired by public discussions and systems proposed by **Andrej Karpathy**, an OpenAI co-founder.

The core idea:

> **Let the model look up your memory the way it looks up Wikipedia.**

The idea quickly gained attention:

- Rapid GitHub growth
- A surge of stars
- Broad discussion across the AI community

**Memory should not depend on context alone. It should be searchable, updateable, and reusable.**

---

## 09 | The Second Brain: Five Layers of Skills

We combined Karpathy's idea with the Obsidian knowledge-base approach to build an:

**AI-Wikipedia-style project memory.**

The backend is built on five integrated Skills:

1. **Everything to Wiki** — turn any content into structured knowledge
2. **Raw to Wiki** — convert source facts into reusable knowledge
3. **Query** — retrieve memory like an encyclopedia
4. **Consistency Check** — find continuity errors, missed changes, broken links, and conflicts
5. **Correction** — fix problems at the source of truth

The result:

> **Even in a one-million-word project,**  
> **the AI can still tell you what weapon a minor character was carrying in Chapter 100.**

**Not clumsy. Not forgetful. The details stay where they belong.**

---

## 10 | The Creative Production Pipeline: Four Steps to a Finished Film

AI filmmaking should not require jumping across a dozen disconnected apps.

Jiucaihezi Studio turns the process into four steps:

1. **Creative Mode + Skills** → Generate production prompts
2. **Creation Panel** → Generate images, video, and audio
3. **Canvas** → Refine the generated material
4. **Creative Mode + Tools** → Assemble the finished film

**Prompt. Generate. Refine. Finish. One connected workflow.**

---

## 11 | Step One: Creative Mode + Skills

Creative Mode and Skills handle the text-production side of the workflow.

Built-in production Skills help break a film into precise, usable prompts:

- Lock the visual style
- Create character prompts
- Create scene prompts
- Create prop prompts
- Create storyboard and shot prompts

This is not generic chat that writes a couple of lines.  
It follows a production workflow and builds the prompts needed for the next stage.

**Define how the film should be made before generating the media.**

---

## 12 | Step Two: The Creation Panel

Once the prompts are ready, move directly into the Creation Panel.

In the same system, you can:

- Select models from China or around the world
- Generate images
- Generate video
- Generate audio

No copying prompts into another app.  
No switching accounts, interfaces, or histories.

**Think on one side. Generate on the other.**

---

## 13 | Step Three: Canvas Refinement

Generated material is not automatically production-ready.

The Canvas lets you:

- Edit images with AI
- Adjust, repair, extend, and align details
- Keep the usable material and correct what is wrong

Your media does not disappear after download.  
It stays inside the project for continued use.

**Generation is the starting point. Refinement makes it production-ready.**

---

## 14 | Step Four: Creative Mode + Tools

After refinement, bring the images, video, and audio back into Creative Mode.

Use the toolchain to:

- Assemble media with tools such as FFmpeg
- Combine assets into a finished film
- Complete the final production steps inside the same project

This is not just a media generator.  
It is a creative system that takes the work all the way to the finish line.

**From prompt to media, from refinement to final cut.**  
**Four steps. One closed loop.**

---

## 15 | The Next Step: Simple Form-Based Workflows

The core system is already in place:

**Creative Mode + Creation Panel + Canvas + Production Tools.**

The next step is making these capabilities easier for beginners to use:

- **E-commerce Mode** — already available; fill out a simple form and generate
- **Manhua / Novel / Film / TV Modes** — planned as scenario-based workflows

Users will not need to understand the underlying Skills.  
Choose a scenario, fill out the form, and get a result.

**Professional power underneath. Simple entry points on top.**

---

## 16 | Comparison

| | Official Single-Model Products | Coding Agents | Direct Model Chat | **Jiucaihezi Studio** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Model access | One company at a time | Development-focused | One model at a time | **One account, many models** |
| Access from mainland China | Difficult | Often more difficult | Difficult | **Low-friction access** |
| Creative structure | Limited | Strong, but code-oriented | Minimal | **Creative Mode with structure** |
| Skills / MCP / tools | Difficult to unify | Available, but code-focused | Limited or unavailable | **Built for creative work** |
| Project memory | Usually absent | Forgets; project memory is engineering-focused | Highly forgetful | **Five-layer Second Brain** |
| Film production workflow | Fragmented | Not built for it | Not built for it | **Four steps to a finished film** |

**Others give you model access. We give you a complete creative system.**

---

## 17 | What You Get with Jiucaihezi Studio

1. **One account for leading models from China and around the world**
2. **Low-friction access to overseas models from mainland China**
3. **Creative Mode with speed, tools, and structure**
4. **A Second Brain that remembers details across million-word projects**
5. **A four-step pipeline: prompts → media → refinement → final cut**
6. **Desktop App + Web, ready wherever you work**
7. **Simple form-based workflows for beginners, coming next**

**Less switching. Less forgetting. Less fragmentation. More finished work.**

---

## 18 | Our Vision

AI should not belong only to people who understand code, models, or design.

Jiucaihezi Studio has a simple goal:

**If you can speak, you can use the best AI.**

We are starting with creative work.  
The larger goal is to make AI useful in everyone’s work and everyday life.

**Not built for a small group of experts.  
Built so the majority of people can finally use AI.**

Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.

---

# GPT Image 2 图像生成专家

你是 GPT Image 2（gpt-image-2）图像生成提示词工程专家。你的核心能力是基于 30+ 分类、162+ 精选提示词的 Reference Gallery，帮用户精准匹配模板、填充变量、并输出生产级 prompt。

## 工作流程

1. **判需求**：听用户描述 → 确定类别（人像/海报/电商/角色/UI/插画/品牌/游戏/动漫…）
2. **搜图库**：打开 `references/gallery.md`（分类索引），加载对应的 `references/gallery-<category>.md`，阅读实际的 `**Prompt**` 内容
3. **精修 prompt**：如有复杂文字/图表/UI/多面板需求，加载 `references/craft.md` 获取高级技巧
4. **确认关键参数**：展示 1-3 个匹配方向供用户选择，最多问 1 个关键问题（比例？风格？产品名？色调？）
5. **输出完整 prompt**：含主提示词 + 推荐参数（比例/尺寸/质量），必要时附负面提示词
6. **引导生成**：提示用户「复制 prompt → 打开创作面板 → 选择 gpt-image-2 模型 → 粘贴生成」

快速通道：用户直接说「生成 xxx」→ 快速查图库匹配 → 输出 prompt → 引导生成。

## GPT Image 2 参数速查

### 尺寸 / 比例
| 用途 | 推荐比例/尺寸 |
|------|--------------|
| 社交正方图 | 1:1 / 1024×1024 |
| 手机竖版（小红书/TikTok） | 9:16 / portrait |
| 手机横版 | 16:9 / landscape |
| 海报/杂志 | 3:4 / portrait |
| 宽屏/桌面壁纸 | 16:9 / wide |
| 打印/论文配图 | 2K / 4K |

### 质量策略
- **探索阶段**：用简短 prompt 快速出图，多看变体
- **最终输出**：prompt 要详尽（材质、光影、构图、色彩、氛围），选高质量
- **中文文字/海报/UI/图表**：必须高质量 + 详细描述文字内容和排版

### 支持的能力
- 文生图：描述 → 图片
- 图生图/编辑：上传参考图 + 文字指令修改
- 批量生成：一次生成多张
- 透明背景：可请求 PNG 透明输出

## Reference Gallery 图库索引

完整的 162+ 精选提示词按类别存放在 `references/` 目录：

| 文件 | 类别 |
|------|------|
| `gallery.md` | **总索引**（先看这个！） |
| `craft.md` | **提示词工艺手册**（文字/图表/UI/多面板高级技巧） |
| `gallery-anime-and-manga.md` | 🎌 动漫 & 漫画 |
| `gallery-gaming.md` | 🎮 游戏画面 |
| `gallery-retro-and-cyberpunk.md` | 🤖 复古 & 赛博朋克 |
| `gallery-cinematic-and-animation.md` | 🎬 电影感 & 动画 |
| `gallery-character-design.md` | 👤 角色设计 |
| `gallery-typography-and-posters.md` | 📝 排版 & 海报 |
| `gallery-illustration.md` | 🎨 插画 |
| `gallery-watercolor.md` | 💧 水彩 |
| `gallery-ink-and-chinese.md` | 🖌️ 水墨 & 国风 |
| `gallery-pixel-art.md` | 🕹️ 像素艺术 |
| `gallery-isometric.md` | 📐 等距视图 |
| `gallery-product-and-food.md` | 📦 产品 & 食品 |
| `gallery-brand-systems-and-identity.md` | 🧩 品牌 VI 系统 |
| `gallery-photography.md` | 📷 摄影 |
| `gallery-screen-photography.md` | 🖥️ 屏幕摄影 |
| `gallery-infographics-and-field-guides.md` | 📊 信息图 & 百科 |
| `gallery-research-paper-figures.md` | 📚 论文配图 |
| `gallery-official-openai-cookbook-examples.md` | 🏢 OpenAI 官方示例 |
| `gallery-edit-endpoint-showcase.md` | ✨ 图片编辑示例 |
| `gallery-ui-ux-mockups.md` | 📱 UI/UX 原型 |
| `gallery-data-visualization.md` | 📊 数据可视化 |
| `gallery-technical-illustration.md` | ⚙️ 技术插图 |
| `gallery-architecture-and-interior.md` | 🏛️ 建筑 & 室内 |
| `gallery-scientific-and-educational.md` | 🔬 科学 & 教育 |
| `gallery-fashion-editorial.md` | 👗 时尚编辑 |
| `gallery-fine-art-painting.md` | 🎨 纯艺绘画 |
| `gallery-more-illustration-styles.md` | ✏️ 更多插画风格 |
| `gallery-cinematic-film-references.md` | 🎥 电影参考 |
| `gallery-beauty-and-lifestyle.md` | 💄 美妆 & 生活方式 |
| `gallery-events-and-experience.md` | 🎟️ 活动 & 体验 |
| `gallery-tattoo-design.md` | 🖋️ 纹身设计 |

加载策略：先看 `gallery.md` 找到对应分类，再加载 1 个分类文件；混合需求加载 2-3 个。不要默认加载全部分类文件。

## 如何生成图片

韭菜盒子内置了 gpt-image-2 模型通道：
1. 打开**创作面板**（左侧导航 "创作"）
2. 选择模型 **gpt-image-2**
3. 粘贴生成的 prompt
4. 设置尺寸/比例/质量等参数
5. 点击生成

或者：直接在对话中说「帮我把这个 prompt 生成了」，如果当前对话支持调用创作工具。

## 来源与致谢

本Skill的提示词图库搬运自 [wuyoscar/GPT-Image2-Skill](https://github.com/wuyoscar/GPT-Image2-Skill)（MIT 协议），原始提示词来自社区创作者在 X (Twitter)、Reddit、OpenAI Cookbook 等平台公开分享的作品。保留 `Curated` vs `Author + Source` 元数据。

## 指令

```commands
人像写真: 请用 GPT Image 图库帮我生成人像写真：
需求：[性别/年龄/风格]
比例：[1:1/9:16]
从图库匹配最佳提示词模板，确认光线/背景/色调后输出完整 prompt。
海报设计: 请用 GPT Image 图库帮我设计海报：
主题：[电影/活动/促销]
标题文字：[标题内容]
比例：[3:4/9:16]
从排版海报图库匹配含文字布局技巧的模板。
角色设计: 请用 GPT Image 图库帮我设计角色：
类型：[游戏/动漫/小说]
描述：[身份/性格/外观]
风格：[日系/美式/国风/写实]
输出含服装/配饰/姿态/光影的完整 prompt。
电商主图: 请用 GPT Image 图库帮我生成电商产品图：
产品：[产品名/类型]
风格：[白底棚拍/场景展示]
比例：[1:1]
输出含材质/光影/构图的完整 prompt。
UI 原型: 请用 GPT Image 图库帮我生成 UI 原型：
类型：[App/网页/Dashboard]
用途：[描述功能]
从 UI/UX 图库匹配模板，输出高质量 prompt。
```

3: # 韭菜盒子 Studio
4: 
5: ## 创作 Agent 产品介绍
6: 
7: **封面金句**  
8: 创作的人，终于不用在「浪费」和「失忆」之间二选一。  
9: 也终于不用为每一个模型，再下载一套产品。
10: 
11: ---
12: 
13: ## 01｜先把今天的创作现实说清楚
14: 
15: 现在几乎所有内容团队都在用 AI。  
16: 问题不在「用没用」，而在**怎么用**。
17: 
18: 现实里，创作者同时卡在两道死结上。
19: 
20: ### 死结一：能力路径二选一，都很疼
21: 
22: **路 A：用编程 Agent 硬扛创作**  
23: Codex、Claude Code 一类工具，能力很强。  
24: 但它们天生为代码仓库设计：
25: 
26: - 为了「稳」，大量读写、反复确认、上下文堆叠  
27: - Token 烧得快  
28: - 换到剧本、角色、分镜、世界观时，路径笨拙  
29: - 细节记得住工程结构，却很难像主创一样记住「这部戏的人设真相」
30: 
31: **强，但贵；能跑，但不像创作者。**
32: 
33: **路 B：直接跟大模型聊**  
34: GPT、Claude Opus、Gemini、DeepSeek……打开就写，确实省。  
35: 但省下来的代价是：
36: 
37: - 聊完就淡  
38: - 角色前后漂移  
39: - 设定散落在聊天记录里  
40: - 上一集定过的东西，下一集要重新喂  
41: - 项目越长，越像在跟一个聪明但健忘的助理反复对齐
42: 
43: **快，但飘；省，但失忆。**
44: 
45: > **要么笨拙地贵，要么轻巧地忘。**
46: 
47: ### 死结二：模型越强，工具越碎
48: 
49: 今天想用 Grok，得进 Grok 的产品。  
50: 想用 GPT，得进 OpenAI 体系：ChatGPT、Codex……  
51: 想用 Claude，得进 Anthropic 那一套。  
52: 想用国内模型，再换另一批 App、网页、账号、额度、工作流。
53: 
54: 结果是：
55: 
56: - 账号一堆  
57: - 界面一堆  
58: - 历史一堆  
59: - 资产一堆  
60: - 同一个角色，在 A 工具画完，在 B 工具接着写，在 C 工具再生成视频——**上下文全断**
61: 
62: 模型没有变少，创作却被切碎了。
63: 
64: 所以今天真正的痛点不是「AI 不够聪明」。  
65: 是：
66: 
67: > **能力分散在无数产品里；  
68: > 记忆消散在无数对话框里；  
69: > 生产线断裂在无数切换里。**
70: 
71: ---
72: 
73: ## 02｜韭菜盒子解决的，就是这组死结
74: 
75: **韭菜盒子 Studio**  
76: 面向内容创作的 AI 工作台。  
77: **桌面 APP（macOS / Windows）+ Web 端**。
78: 
79: 它不是又一个聊天框，也不是把编程 Agent 换皮成创作界面。  
80: 它同时做三件关键的事：
81: 
82: 1. **一个账号，打通中外主流模型** —— 不再为每个模型单独换产品  
83: 2. **第二大脑，记住项目细节** —— 不再在「省 token」和「能记住」之间妥协  
84: 3. **完整流水线，从小说到影像** —— 不再把写作、设定、生图、生视频拆成孤岛
85: 
86: 一句话：
87: 
88: > **用户主导，AI 执行。  
89: > 模型负责聪明，系统负责统一入口、长期记忆、全链路落地。**
90: 
91: ---
92: 
93: ## 03｜卖点一：一个账号，中外模型全开
94: 
95: 这是最直观、也最能立刻被感受到的能力。
96: 
97: ### 以前怎么用模型
98: 
99: | 你想用的能力 | 你通常要做的事 |
100: | --- | --- |
101: | Grok | 下载 / 打开 Grok 相关产品 |
102: | GPT | 进入 OpenAI 产品体系（ChatGPT、Codex 等） |
103: | Claude / Opus | 进入对应官方产品 |
104: | Gemini | 再换一套 Google 侧产品 |
105: | 国内模型 | 再开一批国内 App / 网页 / 账号 |
106: 
107: 每一次切换，都不只是换一个 logo。  
108: 是在换：
109: 
110: - 账号体系  
111: - 交互习惯  
112: - 历史记录  
113: - 文件位置  
114: - 项目上下文
115: 
116: ### 韭菜盒子怎么做
117: 
118: **一个账号登录，即可调用中外主流模型。**  
119: 写、画、拍、改，都在同一工作台完成。  
120: 不用为了换模型，再换一整套产品。
121: 
122: 对创作者意味着：
123: 
124: - **选型自由**：哪个模型适合剧本就用哪个，适合生图就用哪个  
125: - **路径统一**：同一项目里切换模型，不离开工作流  
126: - **资产不断**：结果回到同一项目，而不是散落在十个 App  
127: - **团队友好**：不要求每个人装齐全网工具，只要求进入一个系统
128: 
129: > **模型可以很多。  
130: > 创作入口，只需要一个。**
131: 
132: ---
133: 
134: ## 04｜核心引擎：创模式
135: 
136: 创模式是韭菜盒子真正不同于「普通模型聊天」和「编程 Agent」的地方。
137: 
138: 它不是套壳问答，而是一条创作运行时：
139: 
140: 1. **直连大模型决策** —— 用最强模型的判断力，不绕远路  
141: 2. **按需加载专业 Skill** —— 编剧、小说、角色、场景、分镜、媒体提示词……需要哪个上哪个  
142: 3. **读写当前项目** —— 结果落进文件，不是飘在对话框  
143: 4. **生成图片 / 视频 / 音频** —— 媒体任务进同一项目链路  
144: 5. **自动沉淀事实** —— 说过什么、改过什么、生成过什么，可追溯
145: 
146: 所以它既不像编程 Agent 那样为了「工程闭环」把流程做笨；  
147: 也不像裸聊模型那样写完就散。
148: 
149: > **创模式 = 聪明的执行层 + 专业的能力包 + 可沉淀的项目手脚。**
150: 
151: ---
152: 
153: ## 05｜真正的破局点：第二大脑
154: 
155: 第二大脑，是韭菜盒子的命门能力。  
156: 不是 slogan，是工程。
157: 
158: ### 别人为什么会忘？
159: 
160: - 聊天 AI：上下文有限，项目真相不在文件里  
161: - 编程 Agent：记得住仓库，不天然理解「角色圣经 / 场景资产 / 剧情连续性」  
162: - 多产品切换：上下文在工具之间彻底断裂  
163: - 网盘 + 微信 + 文档：人记得住入口，系统接不上
164: 
165: ### 韭菜盒子怎么记？
166: 
167: | 层 | 做什么 | 价值 |
168: | --- | --- | --- |
169: | **项目 Wiki** | 角色 / 场景 / 道具 / 世界 / 剧情结构化沉淀 | 项目有单一真相源 |
170: | **热记忆 hot** | 当前最关键事实优先注入模型 | 每次开聊都先对齐主线 |
171: | **事实账本 .raw** | 用户意图、AI 动作、文件变更可追溯 | 不是“好像说过”，而是有据可查 |
172: | **巡检与修正** | 查穿帮、漏改、断链、设定冲突 | 长项目不靠人肉回忆 |
173: | **对话转 Wiki** | 把过程沉淀成可复用知识 | 聊过的不会白聊 |
174: 
175: 结果是：
176: 
177: > **不笨拙：不必用编程 Agent 的重路径硬扛创作；  
178: > 不健忘：关键细节写进项目，而不是赌模型上下文；  
179: > 不碎片：模型切换发生在系统内，记忆仍在项目里。**
180: 
181: 对影视与内容团队，这意味着：  
182: 一部戏最贵的不是生成一张图，  
183: 是**角色不飘、世界观不散、多集不断、资产可复用。**
184: 
185: ---
186: 
187: ## 06｜完整流水线：从文字到影像
188: 
189: 大多数 AI 工具停在单点：  
190: 会写，或会画，或会动一下。
191: 
192: 韭菜盒子复现的是内容工业真正需要的链路：
193: 
194: ```
195: 灵感 / IP
196:    ↓
197: 小说（长线叙事）
198:    ↓
199: 剧本（分集、成稿、爽点、钩子）
200:    ↓
201: 工程化素材（角色、场景、道具、分镜）
202:    ↓
203: 生图（设定图、空镜、海报、物料）
204:    ↓
205: 生视频（镜头、动态、成片素材）
206:    ↓
207: 第二大脑持续记忆与巡检
208: ```
209: 
210: **不是五个散装功能。**  
211: 是一条能从头跑到尾、并且中途不丢记忆的生产线。  
212: 再叠加「一个账号调用中外模型」，整条链路终于不用在十几个产品之间跳。
213: 
214: ---
215: 
216: ## 07｜五个必须被看见的能力
217: 
218: ### ① 写小说：长线叙事可续写
219: 
220: - 灵感到成稿全流程  
221: - 核心梗、角色网、大纲、章纲、正文、复盘  
222: - 进入 Wiki，可续写、可巡检、可修正
223: 
224: 小说不是一次聊完就散。  
225: 它变成**可生长的 IP 资产。**
226: 
227: ---
228: 
229: ### ② 写剧本：从梗概到可拍摄成稿
230: 
231: - 短剧 / 漫剧 / 爽剧专业流  
232: - 世界观 + 极致角色 + 催化剂  
233: - 分集大纲 → 逐集成稿  
234: - 反转、卡点、钩子、临摹换皮 / 从零涌现
235: 
236: 不是「帮你写个剧情」。  
237: 是按内容生产逻辑组织的**剧本生产线。**
238: 
239: ---
240: 
241: ### ③ 生图：出的是资产，不是灵感截图
242: 
243: - 文生图 / 图生图  
244: - 多模型可选（同一账号内切换）  
245: - 角色设定、场景空镜、道具多视图、海报物料  
246: - 结果回写项目，可进画布继续用
247: 
248: 对团队真正有价值的是：  
249: **可复用、可对齐、可进入下一环节。**
250: 
251: ---
252: 
253: ### ④ 生视频：嵌在项目流里，不是孤立按钮
254: 
255: - 多引擎视频生成  
256: - 图生视频 / 镜头驱动  
257: - 与分镜、角色、场景提示词衔接
258: 
259: 重点不是「能动一下」，  
260: 而是镜头结果仍然活在同一项目记忆里，  
261: 后面还能继续改、继续接、继续生产。
262: 
263: ---
264: 
265: ### ⑤ 第二大脑：让整条流水线不崩
266: 
267: 没有第二大脑，上面四项都会在第二周开始变形。  
268: 有了第二大脑：
269: 
270: - 角色脸和性格不漂  
271: - 场景规则不乱  
272: - 前后集不断档  
273: - 团队换人也能接上
274: 
275: > **第二大脑不是附加功能。  
276: > 它是整条创作流水线的稳定器。**
277: 
278: ---
279: 
280: ## 08｜APP + Web：同一套创作系统
281: 
282: | 端 | 形态 | 价值 |
283: | --- | --- | --- |
284: | **桌面 APP** | macOS / Windows | 全能力工作台：创模式、项目文件、Skill、媒体生成、画布、深度创作 |
285: | **Web 端** | 浏览器即开 | 轻量进入创模式，降低启动成本，随时继续创作 |
286: 
287: 双端共享同一产品哲学：
288: 
289: - 同一个项目思维  
290: - 同一套 Skill 能力  
291: - 同一条媒体任务链路  
292: - 同一个账号下的模型能力  
293: - 同一种「用户主导、AI 执行」
294: 
295: **不是两个产品，是一个创作系统的两种入口。**
296: 
297: ---
298: 
299: ## 09｜为什么这不是套壳
300: 
301: | 维度 | 裸聊大模型 | 编程 Agent | 各家官方单点产品 | **韭菜盒子 Studio** |
302: | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
303: | 创作门槛 | 低 | 高 | 中 | **低（说人话）** |

311: 最关键的三句：
312: 
313: > **别人给你的是模型入口。**  
314: > **韭菜盒子给你的是统一入口 + 长期记忆 + 创作操作系统。**  
315: > **模型再多，也不该逼创作者活在切换里。**

319: ## 10｜对内容团队意味着什么
320: 
321: 1. **不再在「贵且笨」和「省且忘」之间妥协**
322: 
323: 既用得上强模型，又留得住项目细节。
324: 
325: 1. **不再为每个模型单独养一套工具**
326: 
327: 一个账号覆盖中外主流模型，选型在系统内完成。
328: 
329: 1. **从灵感工具升级为生产能力**
330: 
331: 小说、剧本、设定、画面、视频在同一系统内推进。
332: 
333: 1. **长项目第一次真正适合 AI**
334: 
335: 多集、多角色、多场景、多版本，靠第二大脑稳住。

337: 1. **资产可复用，而不是聊天记录可翻找**

339: 项目文件就是真相，知识可以沉淀、巡检、迭代。

343: ## 11｜收束页
344: 
345: **聪明，从来不是创作的全部。**  
346: **统一入口，才配谈效率。**  
347: **记得住，才配谈生产。**

349: 韭菜盒子 Studio
350: 
351: - **一个账号** —— 中外模型统一调用  
352: - **创模式** —— 负责执行

arguments.content:
# Jiucaihezi Studio — English PPT GPT Image 2 Prompts

**Company:** Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.  
**Date:** 2026-07-20  
**Format:** 16:9 widescreen, one generated image per PPT slide  
**Model:** GPT Image 2  
**Visual direction:** Deep-Space Media Pitch Deck

> Each section below is a complete, standalone prompt. Copy one entire section directly into GPT Image 2. Do not combine it with another prompt.

---

## Global Style Lock

Use this visual system on every slide: premium cinematic technology pitch deck, near-black background `#0B0D12`, charcoal-gray modular panels `#161A22`, electric mint accent `#5EEAD4`, restrained amber accent `#F5B942` only for key breakthrough or Second Brain elements, crisp white typography, cool-gray secondary text, subtle cinematic grain, soft volumetric glow, precise Swiss editorial grid, generous negative space, thin technical connector lines, refined product-strategy aesthetic, restrained and expensive, not flashy. Use the same typography, spacing, corner radius, line weight, icon language, header, footer, and page-number system across the full deck. Put “JIUCAIHEZI STUDIO” in the upper-left header and “SHANGHAI CHUNQIAN CULTURE MEDIA CO., LTD.” in the lower-left footer unless the slide is the cover. Use clear, accurate, highly legible American English text exactly as provided. Text must be clean vector-like typography, not distorted, misspelled, handwritten, or decorative. No fake logos, no invented brands, no extra words, no tiny unreadable paragraphs, no cyberpunk overload, no cartoon style, no neon explosion, no generic stock-photo collage. Create a polished investor-grade 16:9 presentation slide, not a poster and not a web page.

---

## Slide 01 — Cover

**Prompt:**

Create a premium 16:9 opening slide for an investor-grade creative technology pitch deck in the locked “Deep-Space Media Pitch Deck” style. Use a near-black cinematic background with a subtle luminous mint network converging into one central, elegant interface-like node. The visual metaphor is simple and powerful: many model paths entering one unified creative system, while a second warm amber memory core remains stable and glowing. Keep the composition bold, minimal, and immediately understandable.

Place this exact large headline in the center-left:

**ONE ACCOUNT. EVERY MODEL.**

Place this exact second headline directly below it, slightly smaller but still prominent:

**A SECOND BRAIN THAT NEVER FORGETS.**

Place “JIUCAIHEZI STUDIO” below the headlines. Place “Desktop App + Web” beneath it in smaller type. Place the exact company name “Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.” discreetly at the bottom-right. Add a small “CREATIVE AGENT” label near the top-right. Use strong visual hierarchy, very large readable typography, generous negative space, and a decisive cinematic glow. No other text.

---

## Slide 02 — One Model, One Product

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium pitch-deck slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Visualize creative work fragmented across separate dark application windows or floating tool panels, each connected to a different isolated model icon-like abstract symbol, with broken mint connector lines and disconnected media cards. The image should communicate friction and fragmentation, not chaos.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE PROBLEM 01**

Large title: **ONE MODEL, ONE PRODUCT.**

Three short lines in a clean left column:
**Want Grok? Use Grok.**
**Want GPT? Go to OpenAI.**
**Want Claude? Switch again.**

A compact right-side consequence stack:
**Too many tools.**
**Too many accounts.**
**Too many disconnected assets.**

Bottom statement in electric mint:
**THE MODELS KEEP GETTING BETTER. THE CREATIVE PROCESS KEEPS GETTING FRAGMENTED.**

Keep all typography large and readable. No extra text and no real company logos.

---

## Slide 03 — Access Barriers

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium cinematic technology pitch slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show a dark, elegant access barrier made of layered verification gates, network lines, a locked account symbol, and abstract payment and identity checkpoints. The mood is frustrating but sophisticated, not political and not alarmist. Use electric mint for the desired destination and muted red-amber warning highlights for obstacles.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE PROBLEM 02**

Large title: **GLOBAL MODELS SHOULDN’T BE THIS HARD TO USE.**

Four concise barrier labels arranged around the visual:
**VPN / ACCESS SETUP**
**OVERSEAS EMAIL**
**IDENTITY VERIFICATION**
**INTERNATIONAL CREDIT CARD**

Small final label near a warning icon:
**ACCOUNT SUSPENSION RISK**

Bottom statement:
**HUGE SETUP COSTS. TOO MANY BARRIERS. CONSTANT UNCERTAINTY.**

Use clean American English, strong grid alignment, and no additional text.

---

## Slide 04 — One Account, Global Model Access

**Prompt:**

Create a bold 16:9 product pitch slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Center the composition on one luminous access point labeled with the product name, with many diverse abstract model nodes from both sides flowing into it and then into one continuous creative project stream. The design must instantly communicate one account and one unified entry point. Use electric mint as the dominant accent and a restrained amber halo around the central system.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE ADVANTAGE**

Large title: **ONE ACCOUNT. GLOBAL MODEL ACCESS.**

Central label: **JIUCAIHEZI STUDIO**

Three short benefit cards:
**SIGN IN ONCE**
**CHOOSE THE RIGHT MODEL**
**STAY IN THE SAME PROJECT**

Bottom statement in large mint type:
**MANY MODELS. ONE CREATIVE ENTRY POINT.**

No fake logos, no extra text, no app-store badges.

---

## Slide 05 — Model Matrix

**Prompt:**

Create a premium 16:9 model-matrix presentation slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Use a clean two-column layout with sophisticated charcoal panels, thin mint dividers, and a subtle constellation of model nodes behind the panels. This is an information slide: prioritize legibility, exact spelling, alignment, and hierarchy over decorative imagery.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **ONE ACCOUNT**

Large title: **LEADING MODELS. ONE PLACE.**

Left panel heading: **LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS**

Left panel text:
**Claude — Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 5 / Fable 5**
**GPT — GPT 5.6, all three modes**
**Grok 4.5**
**Gemini 3.5 Flash**
**DeepSeek Pro**
**GLM 5.2**

Right panel heading: **IMAGE AND VIDEO MODELS**

Right panel text:
**CDA 12.0**
**Grok**
**Sora 2**
**GPT Image 2**
**Banana Pro**
**Plus leading models from China and around the world**

Bottom statement:
**THE MODEL CHOICE STAYS YOURS. THE WORKFLOW STAYS UNIFIED.**

Do not invent model names or add logos. Make every line crisp and readable.

---

## Slide 06 — Both Existing Paths Fall Short

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 split-screen comparison slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Divide the slide into two balanced charcoal panels separated by a narrow vertical gap. Left panel should feel heavy and over-engineered with dense technical traces; right panel should feel fast but unstable with drifting fragments and broken guide lines. Both paths must visibly end in the same muted memory-loss symbol, showing that both are forgetful.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE PROBLEM 03**

Large title: **BOTH EXISTING PATHS FALL SHORT.**

Left panel heading: **CODING AGENTS**
**Burn tokens**
**Still forget details**
**Built around programming**

Left bottom line:
**POWERFUL. HEAVY. FORGETFUL.**

Right panel heading: **DIRECT MODEL CHAT**
**Little structure**
**Easy to drift**
**Weak Skills / MCP / plugin support**

Right bottom line:
**FAST. LOOSE. FORGETFUL.**

Bottom full-width statement in mint:
**ONE PATH IS HEAVY AND FORGETFUL. THE OTHER IS LOOSE AND FORGETFUL.**

No extra text.

---

## Slide 07 — Creative Mode

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium product architecture slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Visualize Creative Mode as a central controlled creative engine: a luminous mint core connected to a project file, Skills, MCP tools, plugins, and media generation modules. The visual balance should combine the speed of direct model access with the structure of a coding agent, without showing code as the main subject.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE SOLUTION**

Large title: **CREATIVE MODE: FAST, STRUCTURED, TOOL-CAPABLE.**

Central label: **CREATIVE MODE**

Five surrounding module labels:
**READ / WRITE PROJECTS**
**CALL SKILLS**
**CALL MCP TOOLS**
**USE PLUGINS**
**GENERATE IMAGE / VIDEO / AUDIO**

Bottom statement:
**THE SPEED OF DIRECT MODEL ACCESS. THE CONTROL OF A REAL WORKFLOW.**

Use clear connector lines and no code blocks, no fake logos, and no extra text.

---

## Slide 08 — The Second Brain: Origin

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 editorial technology slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show an elegant abstract knowledge map resembling an open digital encyclopedia: searchable memory nodes, linked cards, and a glowing query path. Include a subtle silhouette-free reference to a public AI research discussion, but do not create a portrait or a fake screenshot. The mood is intellectual, credible, and foundational. Use amber only on the central memory query.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE SECOND BRAIN 01**

Large title: **LET THE MODEL LOOK UP YOUR MEMORY LIKE WIKIPEDIA.**

Body text:
**Inspired by public systems proposed by Andrej Karpathy, an OpenAI co-founder.**

Three small labels:
**SEARCHABLE**
**UPDATEABLE**
**REUSABLE**

Bottom statement:
**MEMORY SHOULD NOT DEPEND ON CONTEXT ALONE.**

Do not add star counts, dates, logos, or unsupported claims. Keep the exact text highly legible.

---

## Slide 09 — The Second Brain: Five Layers

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium system-diagram slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Build a vertical five-layer architecture with five connected charcoal modules, mint connector lines, and a restrained amber glow passing through the entire stack. The diagram should feel like an AI-Wikipedia knowledge engine for creative projects. Make the five layers visually distinct and easy to scan.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE SECOND BRAIN 02**

Large title: **FIVE SKILLS. ONE PROJECT MEMORY.**

Five numbered layers:
**01 EVERYTHING TO WIKI**
**02 RAW TO WIKI**
**03 QUERY**
**04 CONSISTENCY CHECK**
**05 CORRECTION**

Small subtitle beneath the stack:
**AN AI-WIKIPEDIA-STYLE KNOWLEDGE BASE**

Bottom statement:
**A ONE-MILLION-WORD PROJECT CAN STILL REMEMBER THE WEAPON IN CHAPTER 100.**

No extra labels or decorative paragraphs. Prioritize diagram clarity and exact typography.

---

## Slide 10 — Four-Step Pipeline Overview

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 cinematic process overview slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show a clean horizontal four-step production pipeline from left to right, with four large connected stations and a subtle film-strip or timeline motif. Each station should have a simple abstract visual: prompt architecture, media generation, canvas refinement, and final film assembly. Use mint for the active flow and amber only at the final finished-film endpoint.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE CREATIVE PRODUCTION PIPELINE**

Large title: **FOUR STEPS TO A FINISHED FILM.**

Four stations:
**01 PROMPT**
**Creative Mode + Skills**

**02 GENERATE**
**Creation Panel**

**03 REFINE**
**Canvas**

**04 FINISH**
**Creative Mode + Tools**

Bottom statement:
**PROMPT. GENERATE. REFINE. FINISH. ONE CONNECTED WORKFLOW.**

No extra text.

---

## Slide 11 — Step One: Prompt

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium workflow detail slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show Creative Mode as a focused control desk connected to precise production Skills. Around it, show five neat prompt cards for visual style, characters, scenes, props, and storyboards. The image should communicate planning and production precision before generation, not generic chatting.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **STEP 01 / PROMPT**

Large title: **CREATIVE MODE + SKILLS.**

Short subtitle:
**TURN A CREATIVE IDEA INTO PRODUCTION-READY PROMPTS.**

Five cards:
**VISUAL STYLE**
**CHARACTERS**
**SCENES**
**PROPS**
**STORYBOARDS / SHOTS**

Bottom statement:
**DEFINE HOW THE FILM SHOULD BE MADE BEFORE GENERATING THE MEDIA.**

Use mint connector lines, clear typography, and no additional text.

---

## Slide 12 — Step Two: Generate

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium product-interface concept slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show a refined Creation Panel with a prompt area on the left, a model selector in the middle, and three clean output cards on the right representing image, video, and audio. This is a conceptual interface diagram, not a literal software screenshot. Keep it spacious and legible.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **STEP 02 / GENERATE**

Large title: **THE CREATION PANEL.**

Short subtitle:
**TURN ONE PRODUCTION PROMPT INTO MEDIA.**

Three output labels:
**IMAGE**
**VIDEO**
**AUDIO**

Small interface label:
**CHOOSE ANY AVAILABLE MODEL**

Bottom statement:
**NO COPY-PASTE. NO APP SWITCHING. JUST GENERATE.**

Use electric mint for active controls and subtle amber for the selected output. No fake logos and no extra UI text.

---

## Slide 13 — Step Three: Refine

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 cinematic editing-workflow slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show a large canvas containing a generated film frame, surrounded by subtle AI editing handles, correction layers, masks, and alignment guides. The result should feel like controlled creative refinement, not a traditional editing timeline. Use a cool mint correction path and a small amber “approved” glow.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **STEP 03 / REFINE**

Large title: **THE CANVAS.**

Short subtitle:
**GENERATION IS THE STARTING POINT. REFINEMENT MAKES IT USABLE.**

Three concise labels:
**EDIT WITH AI**
**REPAIR DETAILS**
**ALIGN THE LOOK**

Bottom statement:
**KEEP THE GOOD. FIX WHAT IS WRONG.**

No extra text, no fake application logo, no clutter.

---

## Slide 14 — Step Four: Finish

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium cinematic post-production slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show refined image, video, and audio assets flowing back into a controlled Creative Mode toolchain, then assembling into one finished film timeline or final-screen frame. Include an abstract FFmpeg tool node as a small technical component, but do not use a fake logo or detailed code. The final endpoint should have a restrained amber glow.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **STEP 04 / FINISH**

Large title: **CREATIVE MODE + TOOLS.**

Short subtitle:
**BRING THE ASSETS BACK. ASSEMBLE THE FINISHED FILM.**

Three small labels:
**IMAGE**
**VIDEO**
**AUDIO**

Tool label:
**FFMPEG / PRODUCTION TOOLS**

Bottom statement:
**FROM PROMPT TO MEDIA. FROM REFINEMENT TO FINAL CUT.**

No extra text and no fake software branding.

---

## Slide 15 — Simple Form-Based Workflows

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium product-roadmap slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Show a sophisticated layered interface concept: powerful professional creative infrastructure underneath, with a simple friendly form-based entry layer above it. Highlight an available e-commerce workflow and show future scenario cards for manhua, novel, film, and TV. Make the future items visibly marked as planned, not already available.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE NEXT STEP**

Large title: **PROFESSIONAL POWER. SIMPLE ENTRY POINTS.**

Central statement:
**NO NEED TO UNDERSTAND THE SKILLS. JUST CHOOSE A SCENARIO AND FILL OUT THE FORM.**

Scenario cards:
**E-COMMERCE — AVAILABLE**
**MANHUA — PLANNED**
**NOVEL — PLANNED**
**FILM — PLANNED**
**TV — PLANNED**

Bottom statement:
**THE CORE SYSTEM IS ALREADY THERE. THE INTERFACE KEEPS GETTING EASIER.**

No extra text.

---

## Slide 16 — Comparison

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium comparison-table slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Use a dark charcoal table with crisp white text, thin mint grid lines, and one clearly highlighted Jiucaihezi Studio column with a subtle amber-to-mint glow. This slide must prioritize accurate readable text and strong alignment over decorative imagery. Use a clean Swiss editorial layout and enough spacing for every row.

Use this exact table content:

Top-left small label: **WHY THIS IS NOT A WRAPPER**

Large title: **FROM MODEL ACCESS TO A CREATIVE SYSTEM.**

Column headers:
**DIMENSION** | **SINGLE-MODEL PRODUCTS** | **CODING AGENTS** | **DIRECT MODEL CHAT** | **JIUCAIHEZI STUDIO**

Rows:
**Model access** | One company at a time | Development-focused | One model at a time | **One account, many models**
**Access from mainland China** | Difficult | Often more difficult | Difficult | **Low-friction access**
**Creative structure** | Limited | Strong, but code-oriented | Minimal | **Creative Mode with structure**
**Skills / MCP / tools** | Difficult to unify | Available, but code-focused | Limited or unavailable | **Built for creative work**
**Project memory** | Usually absent | Forgets; engineering-focused | Highly forgetful | **Five-layer Second Brain**
**Film production workflow** | Fragmented | Not built for it | Not built for it | **Four steps to a finished film**

Bottom statement:
**OTHERS GIVE YOU MODEL ACCESS. WE GIVE YOU A COMPLETE CREATIVE SYSTEM.**

Do not add rows, logos, footnotes, or extra text.

---

## Slide 17 — What You Get

**Prompt:**

Create a 16:9 premium value-proposition slide in the locked Deep-Space Media style. Use a strong vertical stack of seven concise benefit cards or a clean seven-point constellation around a central glowing project core. The visual should feel calm, confident, and production-oriented. Highlight the Second Brain and the finished-film pipeline with restrained amber accents.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **THE OUTCOME**

Large title: **WHAT YOU GET WITH JIUCAIHEZI STUDIO.**

Seven benefit lines:
**ONE ACCOUNT FOR LEADING MODELS**
**LOW-FRICTION ACCESS FROM MAINLAND CHINA**
**CREATIVE MODE WITH SPEED AND STRUCTURE**
**A SECOND BRAIN THAT REMEMBERS DETAILS**
**A FOUR-STEP PIPELINE TO THE FINAL CUT**
**DESKTOP APP + WEB**
**SIMPLE WORKFLOWS FOR BEGINNERS**

Bottom statement in mint:
**LESS SWITCHING. LESS FORGETTING. MORE FINISHED WORK.**

No extra text.

---

## Slide 18 — Our Vision

**Prompt:**

Create a restrained, warm, human 16:9 closing slide for the locked Deep-Space Media Pitch Deck. Keep the near-black background and premium grid, but soften the cinematic technology glow. Show a wide field of diverse ordinary people connected by one gentle mint light: a student, teacher, doctor, lawyer, delivery worker, small business owner, and independent creator. Do not make it a literal collage; use elegant silhouettes or minimal editorial figures, with no stereotypes and no visible faces required. The emotional tone is humble, inclusive, and sincere.

Use this exact text:

Top-left small label: **OUR VISION**

Large title:
**AI SHOULD BE SIMPLE ENOUGH FOR EVERYONE.**

Short body text:
**If you can speak, you can use the best AI.**

Second line:
**Built for ordinary people—not just experts.**

Bottom statement in electric mint:
**MAKE AI USEFUL IN EVERYONE’S WORK AND EVERYDAY LIFE.**

Bottom-right company name:
**Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.**

Use generous negative space, large readable type, and no additional words.

---

## Recommended GPT Image 2 Settings

- **Aspect ratio:** 16:9 widescreen
- **Quality:** High
- **Style:** Follow the prompt exactly; prioritize legible presentation typography
- **Generation workflow:** Generate Slide 01 first, then Slides 04, 07, 09, 16, and 18 to calibrate the visual system before generating the remaining slides
- **Important:** Do not add any text that is not explicitly included in the slide prompt

<skill_content name="gpt-image">
# Skill: gpt-image

---
name: gpt-image
description: "Use this skill whenever a user asks to generate, create, draw, render, or edit images with GPT Image 2 / gpt-image-2, text-to-image, reference-image editing, inpainting, posters, typography, Chinese text, UI mockups, diagrams, or gallery prompts. Analyze the user's prompt, search the bundled Reference Gallery/craft files for matching design patterns, confer on direction when useful, then call the packaged `gpt-image` CLI or bundled `scripts/generate.py`. Do not write new image-generation code unless explicitly asked to modify this repo."
compatibility: "Requires Python 3.11+ and either `gpt-image`, `uv`, or `uvx`. CLI/API calls read `OPENAI_API_KEY` and may incur OpenAI API charges."
metadata: {"openclaw":{"requires":{"anyBins":["gpt-image","uv","uvx"]},"primaryEnv":"OPENAI_API_KEY","homepage":"https://github.com/wuyoscar/gpt_image_2_skill"}}
---

# gpt-image

Agent runbook for GPT Image 2 generation/editing. Use the prompt library + packaged CLI. Do not reimplement image API code.

## Operating loop

0. **🔑 Key check (ALWAYS FIRST)**: Before ANY image operation, check if `OPENAI_API_KEY` is configured.
   - Check both process env (`echo $OPENAI_API_KEY`) and `~/.env` file.
   - **If key is missing** → immediately ask user via interactive dialog with two questions:
     1. "API Key" (password field) — their NewAPI key
     2. "API Base URL" (text field, default: `https://api.jiucaihezi.studio/v1`)
   - After user submits, write `OPENAI_BASE_URL=...` and `OPENAI_API_KEY=...` to `~/.env`.
     The CLI reads this file automatically. User never touches terminal.
   - **If key exists** → skip to step 1.

1. **Classify request**: `generate`, `edit`, `inpaint`, or `multi-reference`; identify asset type, exact text, aspect ratio, references, safety constraints, and budget/quality.
2. **Search references first**: open `references/gallery.md`; load/search the closest `references/gallery-<category>.md` file(s). Read actual `**Prompt**` text before choosing a pattern.
3. **Refine with craft**: load `references/craft.md` for dense text, diagrams, UI, data visualization, multi-panel layouts, weak prompts, or no close gallery match.
4. **Confer when useful**: before costly/ambiguous/high-polish calls, present 1–3 matched directions plus planned size/quality; ask at most one concise question. Skip long discussion for precise “generate now” requests.
5. **Preflight, no side effects**: use existing CLI/skill if present. Check command availability (`command -v gpt-image`), installed tool lists when the tool manager exists, or the runtime’s own skill registry when available. Do not assume a local home path in cloud/hosted runtimes.
6. **No blind setup**: do not reinstall, overwrite skill folders, create/modify `.env`, or write API keys unless the user explicitly requested setup. Global/shared installs are opt-in only.
7. **Execute via CLI only**: call `gpt-image` or `scripts/generate.py`. Do not create a new `generate.py`, SDK wrapper, or ad-hoc script for normal image requests.
8. **Report**: output file path(s), key flags, and one concise refinement suggestion if useful.

Fast path: precise prompt + explicit “generate now” → quick reference/craft check, then CLI.

## CLI resolution

Preferred call order:

```bash
# Existing CLI on PATH
gpt-image -p "PROMPT" [-f OUT] [-i REF...] [-m MASK] [options]

# Installed skill folder; use runtime-provided skill path when available
uv run "$SKILL_DIR/scripts/generate.py" -p "PROMPT" [-f OUT] [-i REF...] [-m MASK] [options]

# Direct transient CLI when the user requested setup/one-off CLI execution
uvx --from git+https://github.com/wuyoscar/gpt_image_2_skill gpt-image -p "PROMPT" [options]
```

`scripts/generate.py` is a launcher: repo-local `src/gpt_image_cli` → installed `gpt-image` → PATH `gpt-image` → transient `uvx`/`uv` fallback.

## Key and cost rules

- CLI reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` from process env, then `.env`, then `~/.env` without overriding existing env; successful API calls may bill the user’s OpenAI account.
- If host/runtime has native platform-managed image generation and the user wants that path, use the host tool instead of this CLI.
- If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset, report missing key or use host-native generation when requested; do not write secrets.
- If user wants to avoid local-key use, respect `unset OPENAI_API_KEY`; if a key exists in `.env`/`~/.env`, tell them to remove/rename it for the session rather than working around it.
- Never print secret values.

## Flags

| Flag | Values | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `-p, --prompt` | string | Required prompt/edit instruction |
| `-f, --file` | path | Output path; auto-named if omitted |
| `-i, --image` | repeatable path | Use edits endpoint; supports multiple references |
| `-m, --mask` | PNG path | Inpaint with alpha mask; requires `-i` |
| `--model` | default `gpt-image-2` | Image model |
| `--size` | `1k`, `2k`, `4k`, `portrait`, `landscape`, `square`, `wide`, `tall`, or literal | Canvas size |
| `--quality` | `low`, `medium`, `high`, `auto` | Cost/quality dial |
| `-n, --n` | integer | Number of images |
| `--background` | `auto`, `opaque` | Generation background |
| `--moderation` | `auto`, `low` | Generation moderation setting |
| `--format` | `png`, `jpeg`, `webp` | Output encoding |
| `--compression` | `0-100` | JPEG/WebP compression |
| `--user` | string | Optional end-user identifier |

Quality policy:
- `low`: cheap drafts, broad exploration, many variants.
- `medium`: normal exploration, style probing, balanced cost.
- `high`: final assets, Chinese text, posters, diagrams, UI, paper figures, dense labels.

Size policy:
- default/social square: `1k` / `1024x1024`
- poster/mobile/beauty: `portrait`
- landscape/gameplay/photo: `landscape`
- print/paper figure: `2k`
- widescreen hero: `4k`
- vertical story/banner: `tall`

## Endpoint routing

| Mode | Trigger | Endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| Text-to-image | no `-i` | `/v1/images/generations` |
| Reference edit | one or more `-i` | `/v1/images/edits` |
| Inpaint | `-i` + `-m` | `/v1/images/edits` with mask |

Surface API errors verbatim enough for debugging; exit codes: `0` success, `1` API/refusal, `2` bad args/missing key.

## Reference loading

- `references/gallery.md`: routing index for the 162-prompt Reference Gallery Atlas. Load first.
- `references/gallery-*.md`: concrete prompts, previews, paths, metadata, attribution. Load 1 category for normal requests; 2–3 for hybrids.
- `references/craft.md`: prompt-craft checklist. Load for prompt repair, exact text, UI/data/diagram grammar, edit invariants, and multi-panel consistency.
- `references/openai-cookbook.md`: official parameter/model semantics. Load for API behavior or model capability questions.

Reference loading policy: load the smallest useful slice; never load all category files by default.

## Verification

- Before API call: confirm endpoint mode, size, quality, output path, and required reference/mask files.
- After CLI call: report path(s) printed by the CLI and surface stderr on failure.
- For edits/inpaints: verify `-i` paths exist; verify `-m` exists when used.

Preserve `Curated` vs `Author + Source` metadata when adapting examples. Add new collected prompts to the Reference Gallery before README promotion.

Create a polished investor-grade 16:9 presentation slide, not a poster and not a web page.

## Slide 19 — Website / Final Call to Explore

**Prompt:**

Use the uploaded product logo image as the primary visual reference. Preserve the logo’s exact identity, proportions, recognizable symbol, colors, and overall visual character. Do not redraw, reinterpret, replace, distort, or invent a new logo. The uploaded logo is a reference asset, not a background image: integrate it cleanly into the slide as the official Jiucaihezi Studio logo.

Create a premium 16:9 final presentation slide in the locked “Deep-Space Media Pitch Deck” style. Use a near-black cinematic background `#0B0D12`, subtle charcoal-gray depth, restrained electric-mint light, soft volumetric glow, precise Swiss editorial grid, generous negative space, and an expensive investor-grade finish. The visual focus is a calm, confident invitation to visit the company website. Do not create a browser screenshot, fake webpage, dashboard, or cluttered product interface. Place the uploaded logo prominently in the center, with generous clear space around it. Under the logo, place this exact product name:

Place the exact company name discreetly at the bottom-right:

**Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.**

Maintain the existing deck’s typography, spacing, corner radius, line weight, and color system. The final feeling should be: clear, trustworthy, welcoming, and quietly powerful. The uploaded logo must remain the only branded graphic element. No fake logos, no additional brands, no distorted lettering, no misspelled text, no tiny unreadable text, no cyberpunk overload, no cartoon style, no neon explosion.

- **Important:** Do not add any text that is not explicitly included in the slide prompt

<skill_content name="pptx">
# Skill: pptx

---
name: pptx
description: "Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions \"deck,\" \"slides,\" \"presentation,\" or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill."
license: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms
---

# PPTX Skill

## Quick Reference

| Task | Guide |
|------|-------|
| Read/analyze content | `python -m markitdown presentation.pptx` |
| Edit or create from template | Read [editing.md](editing.md) |
| Create from scratch | Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) |

---

## Reading Content

```bash
# Text extraction
python -m markitdown presentation.pptx

# Visual overview
python scripts/thumbnail.py presentation.pptx

# Raw XML
python scripts/office/unpack.py presentation.pptx unpacked/
```

---

## Editing Workflow

**Read [editing.md](editing.md) for full details.**

1. Analyze template with `thumbnail.py`
2. Unpack → manipulate slides → edit content → clean → pack

---

## Creating from Scratch

**Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) for full details.**

Use when no template or reference presentation is available.

---

## Design Ideas

**Don't create boring slides.** Plain bullets on a white background won't impress anyone. Consider ideas from this list for each slide.

### Before Starting

- **Pick a bold, content-informed color palette**: The palette should feel designed for THIS topic. If swapping your colors into a completely different presentation would still "work," you haven't made specific enough choices.
- **Dominance over equality**: One color should dominate (60-70% visual weight), with 1-2 supporting tones and one sharp accent. Never give all colors equal weight.
- **Dark/light contrast**: Dark backgrounds for title + conclusion slides, light for content ("sandwich" structure). Or commit to dark throughout for a premium feel.
- **Commit to a visual motif**: Pick ONE distinctive element and repeat it — rounded image frames, icons in colored circles, thick single-side borders. Carry it across every slide.

### Color Palettes

Choose colors that match your topic — don't default to generic blue. Use these palettes as inspiration:

| Theme | Primary | Secondary | Accent |
|-------|---------|-----------|--------|
| **Midnight Executive** | `1E2761` (navy) | `CADCFC` (ice blue) | `FFFFFF` (white) |
| **Forest & Moss** | `2C5F2D` (forest) | `97BC62` (moss) | `F5F5F5` (cream) |
| **Coral Energy** | `F96167` (coral) | `F9E795` (gold) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |
| **Warm Terracotta** | `B85042` (terracotta) | `E7E8D1` (sand) | `A7BEAE` (sage) |
| **Ocean Gradient** | `065A82` (deep blue) | `1C7293` (teal) | `21295C` (midnight) |
| **Charcoal Minimal** | `36454F` (charcoal) | `F2F2F2` (off-white) | `212121` (black) |
| **Teal Trust** | `028090` (teal) | `00A896` (seafoam) | `02C39A` (mint) |
| **Berry & Cream** | `6D2E46` (berry) | `A26769` (dusty rose) | `ECE2D0` (cream) |
| **Sage Calm** | `84B59F` (sage) | `69A297` (eucalyptus) | `50808E` (slate) |
| **Cherry Bold** | `990011` (cherry) | `FCF6F5` (off-white) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |

### For Each Slide

**Every slide needs a visual element** — image, chart, icon, or shape. Text-only slides are forgettable.

**Layout options:**
- Two-column (text left, illustration on right)
- Icon + text rows (icon in colored circle, bold header, description below)
- 2x2 or 2x3 grid (image on one side, grid of content blocks on other)
- Half-bleed image (full left or right side) with content overlay

**Data display:**
- Large stat callouts (big numbers 60-72pt with small labels below)
- Comparison columns (before/after, pros/cons, side-by-side options)
- Timeline or process flow (numbered steps, arrows)

**Visual polish:**
- Icons in small colored circles next to section headers
- Italic accent text for key stats or taglines

### Typography

**Choose an interesting font pairing** — don't default to Arial. Pick a header font with personality and pair it with a clean body font.

| Header Font | Body Font |
|-------------|-----------|
| Georgia | Calibri |
| Arial Black | Arial |
| Calibri | Calibri Light |
| Cambria | Calibri |
| Trebuchet MS | Calibri |
| Impact | Arial |
| Palatino | Garamond |
| Consolas | Calibri |

| Element | Size |
|---------|------|
| Slide title | 36-44pt bold |
| Section header | 20-24pt bold |
| Body text | 14-16pt |
| Captions | 10-12pt muted |

### Spacing

- 0.5" minimum margins
- 0.3-0.5" between content blocks
- Leave breathing room—don't fill every inch

### Avoid (Common Mistakes)

- **Don't repeat the same layout** — vary columns, cards, and callouts across slides
- **Don't center body text** — left-align paragraphs and lists; center only titles
- **Don't skimp on size contrast** — titles need 36pt+ to stand out from 14-16pt body
- **Don't default to blue** — pick colors that reflect the specific topic
- **Don't mix spacing randomly** — choose 0.3" or 0.5" gaps and use consistently
- **Don't style one slide and leave the rest plain** — commit fully or keep it simple throughout
- **Don't create text-only slides** — add images, icons, charts, or visual elements; avoid plain title + bullets
- **Don't forget text box padding** — when aligning lines or shapes with text edges, set `margin: 0` on the text box or offset the shape to account for padding
- **Don't use low-contrast elements** — icons AND text need strong contrast against the background; avoid light text on light backgrounds or dark text on dark backgrounds
- **NEVER use accent lines under titles** — these are a hallmark of AI-generated slides; use whitespace or background color instead

---

## QA (Required)

**Assume there are problems. Your job is to find them.**

Your first render is almost never correct. Approach QA as a bug hunt, not a confirmation step. If you found zero issues on first inspection, you weren't looking hard enough.

### Content QA

```bash
python -m markitdown output.pptx
```

Check for missing content, typos, wrong order.

**When using templates, check for leftover placeholder text:**

```bash
python -m markitdown output.pptx | grep -iE "xxxx|lorem|ipsum|this.*(page|slide).*layout"
```

If grep returns results, fix them before declaring success.

### Visual QA

**⚠️ USE SUBAGENTS** — even for 2-3 slides. You've been staring at the code and will see what you expect, not what's there. Subagents have fresh eyes.

Convert slides to images (see [Converting to Images](#converting-to-images)), then use this prompt:

```
Visually inspect these slides. Assume there are issues — find them.

Look for:
- Overlapping elements (text through shapes, lines through words, stacked elements)
- Text overflow or cut off at edges/box boundaries
- Decorative lines positioned for single-line text but title wrapped to two lines
- Source citations or footers colliding with content above
- Elements too close (< 0.3" gaps) or cards/sections nearly touching
- Uneven gaps (large empty area in one place, cramped in another)
- Insufficient margin from slide edges (< 0.5")
- Columns or similar elements not aligned consistently
- Low-contrast text (e.g., light gray text on cream-colored background)
- Low-contrast icons (e.g., dark icons on dark backgrounds without a contrasting circle)
- Text boxes too narrow causing excessive wrapping
- Leftover placeholder content

For each slide, list issues or areas of concern, even if minor.

Read and analyze these images:
1. /path/to/slide-01.jpg (Expected: [brief description])
2. /path/to/slide-02.jpg (Expected: [brief description])

Report ALL issues found, including minor ones.
```

### Verification Loop

1. Generate slides → Convert to images → Inspect
2. **List issues found** (if none found, look again more critically)
3. Fix issues
4. **Re-verify affected slides** — one fix often creates another problem
5. Repeat until a full pass reveals no new issues

**Do not declare success until you've completed at least one fix-and-verify cycle.**

---

## Converting to Images

Convert presentations to individual slide images for visual inspection:

```bash
python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf output.pptx
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 output.pdf slide
```

This creates `slide-01.jpg`, `slide-02.jpg`, etc.

To re-render specific slides after fixes:

```bash
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 -f N -l N output.pdf slide-fixed
```

---

## Dependencies

- `pip install "markitdown[pptx]"` - text extraction
- `pip install Pillow` - thumbnail grids
- `npm install -g pptxgenjs` - creating from scratch
- LibreOffice (`soffice`) - PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)
- Poppler (`pdftoppm`) - PDF to images

Base directory for this skill: skill://local/pptx

1: # PptxGenJS Tutorial
2: 
3: ## Setup & Basic Structure
4: 
5: ```javascript
6: const pptxgen = require("pptxgenjs");
7: 
8: let pres = new pptxgen();
9: pres.layout = 'LAYOUT_16x9';  // or 'LAYOUT_16x10', 'LAYOUT_4x3', 'LAYOUT_WIDE'
10: pres.author = 'Your Name';
11: pres.title = 'Presentation Title';
12: 
13: let slide = pres.addSlide();
14: slide.addText("Hello World!", { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, fontSize: 36, color: "363636" });
15: 
16: pres.writeFile({ fileName: "Presentation.pptx" });
17: ```
18: 
19: ## Layout Dimensions
20: 
21: Slide dimensions (coordinates in inches):
22: - `LAYOUT_16x9`: 10" × 5.625" (default)
23: - `LAYOUT_16x10`: 10" × 6.25"
24: - `LAYOUT_4x3`: 10" × 7.5"
25: - `LAYOUT_WIDE`: 13.3" × 7.5"
26: 
27: ---
28: 
29: ## Text & Formatting
30: 
31: ```javascript
32: // Basic text
33: slide.addText("Simple Text", {
34:   x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 2, fontSize: 24, fontFace: "Arial",
35:   color: "363636", bold: true, align: "center", valign: "middle"
36: });
37: 
38: // Character spacing (use charSpacing, not letterSpacing which is silently ignored)
39: slide.addText("SPACED TEXT", { x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 1, charSpacing: 6 });
40: 
41: // Rich text arrays
42: slide.addText([
43:   { text: "Bold ", options: { bold: true } },
44:   { text: "Italic ", options: { italic: true } }
45: ], { x: 1, y: 3, w: 8, h: 1 });
46: 
47: // Multi-line text (requires breakLine: true)
48: slide.addText([
49:   { text: "Line 1", options: { breakLine: true } },
50:   { text: "Line 2", options: { breakLine: true } },
51:   { text: "Line 3" }  // Last item doesn't need breakLine
52: ], { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 8, h: 2 });
53: 
54: // Text box margin (internal padding)
55: slide.addText("Title", {
56:   x: 0.5, y: 0.3, w: 9, h: 0.6,
57:   margin: 0  // Use 0 when aligning text with other elements like shapes or icons
58: });
59: ```
60: 
61: **Tip:** Text boxes have internal margin by default. Set `margin: 0` when you need text to align precisely with shapes, lines, or icons at the same x-position.
62: 
63: ---
64: 
65: ## Lists & Bullets
66: 
67: ```javascript
68: // ✅ CORRECT: Multiple bullets
69: slide.addText([
70:   { text: "First item", options: { bullet: true, breakLine: true } },
71:   { text: "Second item", options: { bullet: true, breakLine: true } },
72:   { text: "Third item", options: { bullet: true } }
73: ], { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 8, h: 3 });
74: 
75: // ❌ WRONG: Never use unicode bullets
76: slide.addText("• First item", { ... });  // Creates double bullets
77: 
78: // Sub-items and numbered lists
79: { text: "Sub-item", options: { bullet: true, indentLevel: 1 } }
80: { text: "First", options: { bullet: { type: "number" }, breakLine: true } }
81: ```
82: 
83: ---
84: 
85: ## Shapes
86: 
87: ```javascript
88: slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {
89:   x: 0.5, y: 0.8, w: 1.5, h: 3.0,
90:   fill: { color: "FF0000" }, line: { color: "000000", width: 2 }
91: });
92: 
93: slide.addShape(pres.shapes.OVAL, { x: 4, y: 1, w: 2, h: 2, fill: { color: "0000FF" } });
94: 
95: slide.addShape(pres.shapes.LINE, {
96:   x: 1, y: 3, w: 5, h: 0, line: { color: "FF0000", width: 3, dashType: "dash" }
97: });
98: 
99: // With transparency
100: slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {
101:   x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,
102:   fill: { color: "0088CC", transparency: 50 }
103: });
104: 
105: // Rounded rectangle (rectRadius only works with ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, not RECTANGLE)
106: // ⚠️ Don't pair with rectangular accent overlays — they won't cover rounded corners. Use RECTANGLE instead.
107: slide.addShape(pres.shapes.ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, {
108:   x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,
109:   fill: { color: "FFFFFF" }, rectRadius: 0.1
110: });
111: 
112: // With shadow
113: slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {
114:   x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,
115:   fill: { color: "FFFFFF" },
116:   shadow: { type: "outer", color: "000000", blur: 6, offset: 2, angle: 135, opacity: 0.15 }
117: });
118: ```
119: 
120: Shadow options:
121: 
122: | Property | Type | Range | Notes |
123: |----------|------|-------|-------|
124: | `type` | string | `"outer"`, `"inner"` | |
125: | `color` | string | 6-char hex (e.g. `"000000"`) | No `#` prefix, no 8-char hex — see Common Pitfalls |
126: | `blur` | number | 0-100 pt | |
127: | `offset` | number | 0-200 pt | **Must be non-negative** — negative values corrupt the file |
128: | `angle` | number | 0-359 degrees | Direction the shadow falls (135 = bottom-right, 270 = upward) |
129: | `opacity` | number | 0.0-1.0 | Use this for transparency, never encode in color string |
130: 
131: To cast a shadow upward (e.g. on a footer bar), use `angle: 270` with a positive offset — do **not** use a negative offset.
132: 
133: **Note**: Gradient fills are not natively supported. Use a gradient image as a background instead.
134: 
135: ---
136: 
137: ## Images
138: 
139: ### Image Sources
140: 
141: ```javascript
142: // From file path
143: slide.addImage({ path: "images/chart.png", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });
144: 
145: // From URL
146: slide.addImage({ path: "https://example.com/image.jpg", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });
147: 
148: // From base64 (faster, no file I/O)
149: slide.addImage({ data: "image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo...", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });
150: ```
151: 
152: ### Image Options
153: 
154: ```javascript
155: slide.addImage({
156:   path: "image.png",
157:   x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3,
158:   rotate: 45,              // 0-359 degrees
159:   rounding: true,          // Circular crop
160:   transparency: 50,        // 0-100
161:   flipH: true,             // Horizontal flip
162:   flipV: false,            // Vertical flip
163:   altText: "Description",  // Accessibility
164:   hyperlink: { url: "https://example.com" }
165: });
166: ```
167: 
168: ### Image Sizing Modes
169: 
170: ```javascript
171: // Contain - fit inside, preserve ratio
172: { sizing: { type: 'contain', w: 4, h: 3 } }
173: 
174: // Cover - fill area, preserve ratio (may crop)
175: { sizing: { type: 'cover', w: 4, h: 3 } }
176: 
177: // Crop - cut specific portion
178: { sizing: { type: 'crop', x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 2, h: 2 } }
179: ```
180: 
181: ### Calculate Dimensions (preserve aspect ratio)
182: 
183: ```javascript
184: const origWidth = 1978, origHeight = 923, maxHeight = 3.0;
185: const calcWidth = maxHeight * (origWidth / origHeight);
186: const centerX = (10 - calcWidth) / 2;
187: 
188: slide.addImage({ path: "image.png", x: centerX, y: 1.2, w: calcWidth, h: maxHeight });
189: ```
190: 
191: ### Supported Formats
192: 
193: - **Standard**: PNG, JPG, GIF (animated GIFs work in Microsoft 365)
194: - **SVG**: Works in modern PowerPoint/Microsoft 365
195: 
196: ---
197: 
198: ## Icons
199: 
200: Use react-icons to generate SVG icons, then rasterize to PNG for universal compatibility.
201: 
202: ### Setup
203: 
204: ```javascript
205: const React = require("react");
206: const ReactDOMServer = require("react-dom/server");
207: const sharp = require("sharp");
208: const { FaCheckCircle, FaChartLine } = require("react-icons/fa");
209: 
210: function renderIconSvg(IconComponent, color = "#000000", size = 256) {
211:   return ReactDOMServer.renderToStaticMarkup(
212:     React.createElement(IconComponent, { color, size: String(size) })
213:   );
214: }
215: 
216: async function iconToBase64Png(IconComponent, color, size = 256) {
217:   const svg = renderIconSvg(IconComponent, color, size);
218:   const pngBuffer = await sharp(Buffer.from(svg)).png().toBuffer();
219:   return "image/png;base64," + pngBuffer.toString("base64");
220: }

from PIL import Image,ImageOps,ImageDraw
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('/Users/by3/Documents/做ppt/韭菜盒子ppt')
files=sorted(p.glob('*.png'))
thumbs=[]
for i,f in enumerate(files):
 im=Image.open(f).convert('RGB'); im.thumbnail((320,180))
 canvas=Image.new('RGB',(340,220),'white'); canvas.paste(im,((340-im.width)//2,20))
 d=ImageDraw.Draw(canvas); d.text((8,2),str(i+1)+' '+f.name[:34],fill='black')
 thumbs.append(canvas)
out=Image.new('RGB',(340*3,220*((len(thumbs)+2)//3)),(230,230,230))
for i,im in enumerate(thumbs): out.paste(im,((i%3)*340,(i//3)*220))

print('/tmp/jiucaihezi-contact.png')

1: # 韭菜盒子 Studio — 开发哲学
2: 
3: > **最后更新**: 2026-07-18 · **分支**: `main` · **版本**: v1.2.6-dev
4: 
5: ## 0. 先审根因，再修症状
6: 
7: Bug 是症状，不是根因。修之前追溯完整链路：
8: 
9: 1. 追溯全链路：什么改动 → 什么坏了 → 那个改动是为什么加的？
10: 2. 质疑每一环：每一环真的需要存在吗？
11: 3. 如果去掉根因既能修 bug 又能简化代码 → 去掉它。
12: 4. 只有根因确实必要，才修症状。
13: 
14: **根因在哪审？在 Wiki 里审。** 开发前必查知识库，项目所有架构、SDD、排障记录、历史改动都在里面：
15: - 📚 知识库首页 → [docs/wiki/CLAUDE.md](docs/wiki/CLAUDE.md)
16: - 📖 生存手册 → [docs/wiki/学习/AI编程生存手册.md](docs/wiki/学习/AI编程生存手册.md)
17: - 🔥 热缓存 → [docs/wiki/hot.md](docs/wiki/hot.md)
18: 
19: > **反面教材**：不要加 `lastServerPid` 去检测进程重启——让服务端自己告诉你 session 无效（OpenCode 官方就是这么做的）。
20: 
21: ## 1. 开发新功能先抄再写
22: 
23: **搜 GitHub/npm → 翻译 OpenCode → 跨平台 → 不得已才自己写。**
24: 
25: - 搜 npm/GitHub 有没有现成的库或实现
26: - OpenCode 有的照抄（[对照表](docs/wiki/架构/对照表.md)是唯一入口）
27: - 画布查 [LeaferJS](https://github.com/leaferjs/leafer)
28: - M 芯片不是标准，Windows/Intel Mac 也要跑对
29: 
30: 动手前明确假设。有歧义就问。有更简单的方案就说。不清楚就停下来。
31: 
32: ## 2. 极简优先
33: 
34: **最少代码解决问题。不多写任何猜测性的东西。**
35: 
36: - 不写没被要求的功能
37: - 不抽象只使用一次的代码
38: - 不加没被要求的"灵活性"或"可配置性"
39: - 不处理不可能发生的错误
40: - 200 行能写成 50 行就重写
41: 
42: 问自己：「一个资深工程师会觉得这过度设计了吗？」如果是，简化。
43: 
44: ## 3. 精准修改
45: 
46: **只动必须动的。只清理自己搞出来的烂摊子。**
47: 
48: - 不改相邻代码、注释、格式
49: - 不顺手重构没坏的东西
50: - 风格跟现有代码保持一致
51: - 只删你自己的改动造成的死引用（import/变量/函数）
52: - 发现无关的历史死代码，提一嘴就行——别删
53: 
54: 检验标准：每一行改动都能追溯到你的任务。
55: 
56: ## 4. 目标驱动执行
57: 
58: **定义成功标准，循环直到验证通过。**
59: 
60: 把任务转化为可验证的目标：
61: 
62: - 「加校验」→「先为无效输入写测试，再让测试通过」
63: - 「修 Bug」→「先写能复现它的测试，再让测试通过」
64: - 「重构 X」→「确保重构前后测试都通过」
65: 
66: 多步骤任务先列出简短计划：
67: 
68: ```text
69: 1. [步骤] -> 验证：[检查方式]
70: 2. [步骤] -> 验证：[检查方式]
71: 3. [步骤] -> 验证：[检查方式]
72: ```
73: 
74: 强的成功标准让你能独立闭环；弱的标准（「让它能用」）会导致不断澄清。
75: 
76: ## 5. 模型优先
77: 
78: **用户提目标，模型定方法；过程放手，结果守标准。**
79: 
80: - 不把专业判断、工具选择和流程设计推给用户
81: - 模型基于目标与事实，自主选择最合适的路径
82: - 系统提供边界、事实和验收标准，不限制模型的合理发挥
83: - 只有目标、事实或权限根本不明确时才提问
84:

1: # Jiucaihezi Studio
2: 
3: **Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.**  
4: **2026-07-20**
5: 
6: > Each section in this document is one PPT slide.  
7: > If it does not appear on the PPT, it does not belong in this document.
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## 01 | Cover
12: 
13: **One account. Every model.**  
14: **A second brain that never forgets.**
15: 
16: Jiucaihezi Studio  
17: Desktop App + Web
18: 
19: Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## 02 | The Problem: One Model, One Product
24: 
25: Want Grok? Use Grok.  
26: Want GPT? Go to OpenAI.  
27: Want Claude? Switch again.  
28: Want China-based models? Open another set of apps and accounts.
29: 
30: The result:
31: 
32: - Too many tools
33: - Too many accounts
34: - Too many histories
35: - Too many disconnected assets
36: 
37: **The models keep getting better. The creative process keeps getting fragmented.**
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 03 | The Problem: Global Models Are Hard to Access from Mainland China
42: 
43: For users in mainland China, reliably using overseas models often means:
44: 
45: - A VPN or other access setup
46: - An overseas email address
47: - Identity verification
48: - An international credit card
49: - The ongoing risk of account suspension
50: 
51: **Huge setup costs. Too many barriers. Constant uncertainty.**
52: 
53: ---
54: 
55: ## 04 | The Advantage: One Account, Global Model Access
56: 
57: **Sign in once.**  
58: Access leading models from China and around the world in one system.
59: 
60: - No switching between apps
61: - No switching between accounts
62: - No rebuilding your setup for every model
63: 
64: **There can be many models. There only needs to be one entry point.**
65: 
66: ---
67: 
68: ## 05 | Model Matrix: Leading Models in One Place
69: 
70: ### Large Language Models
71: 
72: - Claude: Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 5 / Fable 5
73: - GPT: GPT 5.6 — all three modes
74: - Grok 4.5
75: - Gemini 3.5 Flash
76: - DeepSeek Pro
77: - GLM 5.2
78: 
79: ### Image and Video Models
80: 
81: - CDA 12.0
82: - Grok
83: - Sora 2
84: - GPT Image 2
85: - Banana Pro
86: - Plus leading models from China and around the world
87: 
88: **Leading global and Chinese models, available through one account.**
89: 
90: ---
91: 
92: ## 06 | The Problem: Both Existing Paths Fall Short
93: 
94: Most people use AI for creative work in one of two ways.
95: 
96: ### Path A | Coding Agents
97: 
98: - Burn through tokens
99: - Still forget important details
100: - Built around programming, not creative production
101: 
102: **Powerful, heavy, expensive—and still forgetful.**
103: 
104: ### Path B | Direct Model Chat
105: 
106: - Little structure or constraint
107: - Easy to drift off track
108: - Weak support for Skills, MCP, and plugins
109: - Less capability, with the same memory problem
110: 
111: **Fast, loose, limited—and still forgetful.**
112: 
113: **One path is heavy and forgetful. The other is loose and forgetful.**
114: 
115: ---
116: 
117: ## 07 | The Solution: Creative Mode
118: 
119: Creative Mode is not a coding agent with a new skin.  
120: It is not a basic chat wrapper.
121: 
122: It combines the best of both approaches:
123: 
124: - **As fast as direct model access**
125: - **As structured and tool-capable as a coding agent**
126: 
127: Creative Mode can:
128: 
129: - Read and write to the current project
130: - Call Skills
131: - Call MCP tools
132: - Use plugins and other tools
133: - Directly generate images, videos, and audio
134: 
135: **Fast, but not uncontrolled. Powerful, but not clumsy.**
136: 
137: ---
138: 
139: ## 08 | The Second Brain: Where the Idea Came From
140: 
141: The Second Brain concept is inspired by public discussions and systems proposed by **Andrej Karpathy**, an OpenAI co-founder.
142: 
143: The core idea:
144: 
145: > **Let the model look up your memory the way it looks up Wikipedia.**
146: 
147: The idea quickly gained attention:
148: 
149: - Rapid GitHub growth
150: - A surge of stars
151: - Broad discussion across the AI community
152: 
153: **Memory should not depend on context alone. It should be searchable, updateable, and reusable.**
154: 
155: ---
156: 
157: ## 09 | The Second Brain: Five Layers of Skills
158: 
159: We combined Karpathy's idea with the Obsidian knowledge-base approach to build an:
160: 
161: **AI-Wikipedia-style project memory.**
162:

<skill_content name="obsidian-markdown">
# Skill: obsidian-markdown

---
name: obsidian-markdown
description: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.
---

# Obsidian Flavored Markdown Skill

Create and edit valid Obsidian Flavored Markdown. Obsidian extends CommonMark and GFM with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, comments, and other syntax. This skill covers only Obsidian-specific extensions -- standard Markdown (headings, bold, italic, lists, quotes, code blocks, tables) is assumed knowledge.

## Workflow: Creating an Obsidian Note

1. **Add frontmatter** with properties (title, tags, aliases) at the top of the file. See [PROPERTIES.md](references/PROPERTIES.md) for all property types.
2. **Write content** using standard Markdown for structure, plus Obsidian-specific syntax below.
3. **Link related notes** using wikilinks (`[[Note]]`) for internal vault connections, or standard Markdown links for external URLs.
4. **Embed content** from other notes, images, or PDFs using the `![[embed]]` syntax. See [EMBEDS.md](references/EMBEDS.md) for all embed types.
5. **Add callouts** for highlighted information using `> [!type]` syntax. See [CALLOUTS.md](references/CALLOUTS.md) for all callout types.
6. **Verify** the note renders correctly in Obsidian's reading view.

> When choosing between wikilinks and Markdown links: use `[[wikilinks]]` for notes within the vault (Obsidian tracks renames automatically) and `[text](url)` for external URLs only.

## Internal Links (Wikilinks)

```markdown
[[Note Name]]                          Link to note
[[Note Name|Display Text]]             Custom display text
[[Note Name#Heading]]                  Link to heading
[[Note Name#^block-id]]                Link to block
[[#Heading in same note]]              Same-note heading link
```

Define a block ID by appending `^block-id` to any paragraph:

```markdown
This paragraph can be linked to. ^my-block-id
```

For lists and quotes, place the block ID on a separate line after the block:

```markdown
> A quote block

^quote-id
```

## Embeds

Prefix any wikilink with `!` to embed its content inline:

```markdown
![[Note Name]]                         Embed full note
![[Note Name#Heading]]                 Embed section
![[image.png]]                         Embed image
![[image.png|300]]                     Embed image with width
![[document.pdf#page=3]]               Embed PDF page
```

See [EMBEDS.md](references/EMBEDS.md) for audio, video, search embeds, and external images.

## Callouts

```markdown
> [!note]
> Basic callout.

> [!warning] Custom Title
> Callout with a custom title.

> [!faq]- Collapsed by default
> Foldable callout (- collapsed, + expanded).
```

Common types: `note`, `tip`, `warning`, `info`, `example`, `quote`, `bug`, `danger`, `success`, `failure`, `question`, `abstract`, `todo`.

See [CALLOUTS.md](references/CALLOUTS.md) for the full list with aliases, nesting, and custom CSS callouts.

## Properties (Frontmatter)

```yaml
---
title: My Note
date: 2024-01-15
tags:
  - project
  - active
aliases:
  - Alternative Name
cssclasses:
  - custom-class
---
```

Default properties: `tags` (searchable labels), `aliases` (alternative note names for link suggestions), `cssclasses` (CSS classes for styling).

See [PROPERTIES.md](references/PROPERTIES.md) for all property types, tag syntax rules, and advanced usage.

## Tags

```markdown
#tag                    Inline tag
#nested/tag             Nested tag with hierarchy
```

Tags can contain letters, numbers (not first character), underscores, hyphens, and forward slashes. Tags can also be defined in frontmatter under the `tags` property.

## Comments

```markdown
This is visible %%but this is hidden%% text.

%%
This entire block is hidden in reading view.
%%
```

## Obsidian-Specific Formatting

```markdown
==Highlighted text==                   Highlight syntax
```

## Math (LaTeX)

```markdown
Inline: $e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0$

Block:
$$
\frac{a}{b} = c
$$
```

## Diagrams (Mermaid)

````markdown
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start] --> B{Decision}
    B -->|Yes| C[Do this]
    B -->|No| D[Do that]
```
````

To link Mermaid nodes to Obsidian notes, add `class NodeName internal-link;`.

## Footnotes

```markdown
Text with a footnote[^1].

[^1]: Footnote content.

Inline footnote.^[This is inline.]
```

## Complete Example

````markdown
---
title: Project Alpha
date: 2024-01-15
tags:
  - project
  - active
status: in-progress
---

# Project Alpha

This project aims to [[improve workflow]] using modern techniques.

> [!important] Key Deadline
> The first milestone is due on ==January 30th==.

## Tasks

- [x] Initial planning
- [ ] Development phase
  - [ ] Backend implementation
  - [ ] Frontend design

## Notes

The algorithm uses $O(n \log n)$ sorting. See [[Algorithm Notes#Sorting]] for details.

![[Architecture Diagram.png|600]]

Reviewed in [[Meeting Notes 2024-01-10#Decisions]].
````

## References

- [Obsidian Flavored Markdown](https://help.obsidian.md/obsidian-flavored-markdown)
- [Internal links](https://help.obsidian.md/links)
- [Embed files](https://help.obsidian.md/embeds)
- [Callouts](https://help.obsidian.md/callouts)
- [Properties](https://help.obsidian.md/properties)

Base directory for this skill: skill://local/obsidian-markdown

---
title: Jiucaihezi Studio — Product Overview
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - product
  - creative-agent
