11: ---
12: 
13: # Jiucaihezi Studio
14: ## Product Overview
15: 
16: **Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.**  
17: **Web + Desktop App**  
18: **Website:** [jiucaihezi.studio](https://jiucaihezi.studio/)
19: 
20: ---
21: 
22: ## 1. What Is Jiucaihezi Studio?
23: 
24: Jiucaihezi Studio is an AI-powered creative workspace designed to help people move from ideas to finished creative work.
25: 
26: It brings models, creative workflows, project memory, media generation, editing, and production tools into one system.
27: 
28: The product is built around two core promises:
29: 
30: > **One account. Every model.**  
31: > **A Second Brain that never forgets.**
32: 
33: This is not simply another chatbot, image generator, or coding agent. It is a **Creative Agent** designed for continuous, structured creative production.
34: 
35: Users can work with leading models from China and around the world without constantly switching between separate products, accounts, interfaces, and histories.
36: 
37: ---
38: 
39: ## 2. Why Existing AI Workflows Break Down
40: 
41: AI has become part of everyday creative work, but the current experience is still fragmented.
42: 
43: ### One model usually means one product
44: 
45: Want to use Grok? Open Grok.  
46: Want GPT? Go to an OpenAI product.  
47: Want Claude? Switch to another platform.  
48: Want a China-based model? Open another app, website, or account.
49: 
50: Every switch can break the workflow:
51: 
52: - Different accounts
53: - Different interfaces
54: - Different billing systems
55: - Different histories
56: - Disconnected files and assets
57: - No shared project memory
58: 
59: The models keep getting better, but the creative process keeps getting split apart.
60: 
61: ### Accessing overseas models can also be difficult
62: 
63: For users in mainland China, accessing overseas AI products may require:
64: 
65: - A VPN or other network setup
66: - An overseas email address
67: - Identity verification
68: - An international payment method
69: - Ongoing concern about account restrictions or suspension
70: 
71: The setup is time-consuming, uncertain, and difficult for ordinary users.
72: 
73: ### The two common AI paths both have weaknesses
74: 
75: #### Coding Agents
76: 
77: Coding agents are powerful, but they are built around software repositories and programming workflows.
78: 
79: They often:
80: 
81: - Use a heavy read-write-confirmation process
82: - Consume large amounts of tokens
83: - Focus on engineering structure rather than creative structure
84: - Forget creative details even while preserving parts of the code workflow
85: 
86: They are powerful, but heavy, expensive, programming-oriented, and still forgetful.
87: 
88: #### Direct Model Chat
89: 
90: Directly chatting with a model is fast and accessible, but it often lacks the structure needed for a long creative project.
91: 
92: It can:
93: 
94: - Drift away from the intended direction
95: - Lose character, world-building, and continuity details
96: - Provide weak support for Skills, MCP, and plugins
97: - Leave the user responsible for manually organizing the project memory
98: 
99: It is fast, but loose, limited, and still forgetful.
100: 
101: > **The current choice is often between a heavy system that forgets and a lightweight system that also forgets.**
102: 
103: Jiucaihezi Studio is designed to remove that tradeoff.
104: 
105: ---
106: 
107: ## 3. One Account, Leading Models in One Workspace
108: 
109: Jiucaihezi Studio provides one unified entry point for models from China and around the world.
110: 
111: Users can choose the model that best fits the current task without leaving the project or rebuilding the workflow from scratch.
112: 
113: ### Large Language Models
114: 
115: The platform provides access to leading models, including:
116: 
117: - Claude Opus 4.8
118: - Claude Sonnet 5
119: - Claude Fable 5
120: - GPT 5.6, including its three modes
121: - Grok 4.5
122: - Gemini 3.5 Flash
123: - DeepSeek Pro
124: - GLM 5.2
125: - Other leading models from China and around the world
126: 
127: ### Image and Video Models
128: 
129: The platform also brings together image and video generation models, including:
130: 
131: - CDA 12.0
132: - Grok
133: - Sora 2
134: - GPT Image 2
135: - Banana Pro
136: - Other leading image and video models from China and around the world
137: 
138: The point is not simply to collect model names. The point is to make model choice part of one continuous creative workflow.
139: 
140: > **There can be many models. There only needs to be one creative entry point.**
141: 
142: ---
143: 
144: ## 4. Creative Mode: Fast Like Chat, Structured Like an Agent
145: 
146: Creative Mode is the core operating mode of Jiucaihezi Studio.
147: 
148: It is not a coding agent with a different visual interface. It is also not a basic chat wrapper.
149: 
150: Creative Mode combines the speed of direct model access with the structure, permissions, and tool capabilities of an agent.
151: 
152: It can:
153: 
154: - Read and write to the current project
155: - Use built-in Skills
156: - Call MCP tools
157: - Use plugins and other production tools
158: - Organize creative materials inside the project
159: - Generate images, video, and audio through the connected workflow
160: 
161: This makes the system both responsive and controlled:
162: 
163: - The model can respond directly instead of following a heavy programming path
164: - The project can still be read, updated, and organized through defined tools
165: - Creative Skills can guide the work instead of leaving every decision to an unconstrained conversation
166: 
167: > **Fast, but not uncontrolled.  Powerful, but not clumsy.**
168: 
169: ---
170: 
171: ## 5. The Second Brain: Memory That Can Be Looked Up
172: 
173: The idea behind the Second Brain is simple:
174: 
175: > **Let the model look up project memory the way it looks up Wikipedia.**
176: 
177: This concept was strongly discussed in the AI community through the work and public writing of **Andrej Karpathy**, an OpenAI co-founder. The idea quickly attracted widespread attention, rapid GitHub growth, and broad discussion about how AI should manage long-term memory.
178: 
179: The key insight is that memory should not depend only on a model's temporary context window.
180: 
181: Project memory should be:
182: 
183: - Searchable
184: - Structured
185: - Updateable
186: - Reusable
187: - Traceable
188: 
189: Jiucaihezi Studio combines this idea with the knowledge-base approach popularized by tools such as Obsidian to build an AI-Wikipedia-style memory system for creative projects.
190: 
191: The goal is straightforward: the AI should not merely sound as if it remembers. It should be able to retrieve the project's actual source of truth.
192: 
193: ---
194: 
195: ## 6. Five Integrated Skills Behind the Second Brain
196: 
197: The Second Brain is supported by five integrated Skills that work together as one memory system.
198: 
199: ### 1. Everything to Wiki
200: 
201: Turn conversations, documents, scripts, notes, and other material into structured project knowledge.
202: 
203: ### 2. Raw to Wiki
204: 
205: Trace confirmed knowledge back to source facts and preserve the original evidence behind the project memory.
206: 
207: ### 3. Query
208: 
209: Search and retrieve project memory as if consulting an encyclopedia.
210: 
211: ### 4. Consistency Check
212: 
213: Detect continuity errors, missed changes, broken links, naming drift, and conflicts across the project.
214: 
215: ### 5. Correction
216: 
217: Apply verified corrections to the source of truth so the project can continue from a clean, consistent foundation.
218: 
219: Together, these five Skills create a complete memory loop:
220: 
221: > **Capture. Preserve. Retrieve. Check. Correct.**
222: 
223: The result is designed for long-form work. Even in a one-million-word project, the AI can still answer a detail such as:
224: 
225: > What weapon was a minor character carrying in Chapter 100?
226: 
227: That is the difference between a model that merely remembers a conversation and a system that maintains project knowledge.
228: 
229: ---
230: 
231: ## 7. From Story to Finished Film
232: 
233: Jiucaihezi Studio is designed to connect the major stages of AI-assisted creative production.
234: 
235: A long-form project can move through a connected path:
236: 
237: ```text
238: Idea / IP
239:   → Novel and long-form story development
240:   → Script and episode development
241:   → Characters, scenes, props, and storyboards
242:   → Image generation
243:   → Video and audio generation
244:   → Canvas refinement
245:   → Final assembly and production
246: ```
247: 
248: The current core workflow is organized into four connected steps.
249: 
250: ### Step 1 — Creative Mode + Skills
251: 
252: Use Creative Mode and built-in Skills to develop the text and production instructions:
253: 
254: - Lock the visual style
255: - Build character prompts
256: - Build scene prompts
257: - Build prop prompts
258: - Build storyboard and shot prompts
259: - Convert creative decisions into precise, usable generation prompts
260: 
261: ### Step 2 — Creation Panel
262: 
263: Move the prompts into the Creation Panel and select the appropriate model from the unified model system.
264: 
265: Generate:
266: 
267: - Images
268: - Video
269: - Audio
270: 
271: The prompt does not need to be copied into another product. The model does not require a separate account or a separate production history.
272: 
273: ### Step 3 — Canvas Refinement
274: 
275: Use the Canvas to refine the generated material:
276: 
277: - AI image editing
278: - Detail repair
279: - Local changes
280: - Extension and adjustment
281: - Visual alignment
282: 
283: Generation is the starting point. Refinement turns a generated result into usable production material.
284: 
285: ### Step 4 — Creative Mode + Tools
286: 
287: Bring the refined assets back into the project and use Creative Mode with production tools to complete the work.
288: 
289: The toolchain can support tasks such as:
290: 
291: - Combining image, video, and audio assets
292: - Adding subtitles and other production elements
293: - Using tools such as FFmpeg to assemble clips
294: - Building a complete video or film sequence
295: 
296: > **Prompt. Generate. Refine. Assemble.**  
297: > **One connected creative production loop.**
298: 
299: ---
300: 
301: ## 8. Easier Entry Points for Beginners
302: 
303: The core system is built for professional creative work, but not every user should need to understand the underlying Skills.
304: 
305: Jiucaihezi Studio is also developing simple, scenario-based form workflows.
306: 
307: The first example is **E-commerce Mode**, which is already available. Users can fill out a guided form and generate product content without understanding the deeper Skill architecture.
308: 
309: Additional scenario-based workflows are planned for:
310: 
311: - Manhua and short-form drama
312: - Novel writing
313: - Film
314: - Television
315: - Other creative and business scenarios
316: 
317: The product direction is simple:
318: 
319: > **Professional power underneath. Simple entry points on top.**
320: 
321: ---
322: 
323: ## 9. Web and Desktop App
324: 
325: Jiucaihezi Studio is available through two entry points.
326: 
327: ### Web Version
328: 
329: Visit [https://jiucaihezi.studio/](https://jiucaihezi.studio/) to start using the product in a browser.
330: 
331: ### Desktop App
332: 
333: The desktop app provides a dedicated environment for deeper project work and can be downloaded from the product interface.
334: 
335: Both versions are built around the same product system, model access, project structure, and creative workflow.
336: 
337: ---
338: 
339: ## 10. How to Get Started
340: 
341: ### Step 1 — Open the product
342: 
343: Go to:
344: 
345: [https://jiucaihezi.studio/](https://jiucaihezi.studio/)
346: 
347: You can begin through the Web version.
348: 
349: ### Step 2 — Register and sign in
350: 
351: Open **Settings** to create an account or sign in.
352: 
353: ### Step 3 — Choose a model
354: 
355: After signing in, use the model selector in the upper-right corner of the conversation panel.
356: 
357: Choose the model that best fits the task and start working.
358: 
359: ### Step 4 — Choose a mode
360: 
361: Jiucaihezi Studio currently provides three main modes:
362: 
363: - **Chat Mode** — discuss ideas and ask questions without directly operating the computer
364: - **Code Mode** — work with programming tasks and software projects
365: - **Creative Mode** — create and manage scripts, stories, prompts, media, and creative projects
366: 
367: ### Step 5 — Open the creative tools
368: 
369: - Click **Creation Panel** on the left to generate images, video, and audio
370: - Open **Editor** to store and organize scripts, stories, and other project materials
371: - Use the Canvas to refine visual assets
372: - Return to Creative Mode to continue the production workflow
373: 
374: ### Step 6 — Download the desktop app if needed
375: 
376: Open **Settings** and use the **Download App** option in the upper-right corner.
377: 
378: The basic workflow is simple:
379: 
380: > **Sign in. Choose a model. Choose a mode. Start creating.**
381: 
382: ---
383: 
384: ## 11. What Users Get
385: 
386: With Jiucaihezi Studio, users get:
387: 
388: - One account for leading models from China and around the world
389: - A single workspace instead of a scattered collection of AI products
390: - Lower-friction access to overseas models for users in mainland China
391: - Creative Mode with structure, tools, Skills, and project access
392: - A Second Brain that preserves long-term project details
393: - A connected workflow from prompts to media to final assembly
394: - Support for novels, scripts, characters, scenes, props, storyboards, images, video, and audio
395: - Web and desktop access
396: - A simpler path for beginners through scenario-based workflows
397: 
398: The practical result is less switching, less repeated setup, less forgetting, and more finished work.
399: 
400: ---
401: 
402: ## 12. Comparison at a Glance
403: 
404: | Capability | Single-Model Products | Coding Agents | Direct Model Chat | **Jiucaihezi Studio** |
405: | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
406: | Model access | One provider at a time | Development-oriented | Usually one model at a time | **One account, many models** |
407: | Project memory | Usually limited | Engineering-focused | Highly dependent on context | **Structured Second Brain** |
408: | Creative workflow | Fragmented or single-purpose | Code-oriented | Unstructured | **Creative Mode + Skills** |
409: | MCP, plugins, and tools | Difficult to unify | Available, but built around code | Limited | **Connected to creative work** |
410: | Image, video, and audio | Often separate products | Not the core focus | Usually disconnected | **One project workflow** |
411: | Long-form continuity | Weak | Not designed for story continuity | Easy to lose details | **Query, check, and correction loop** |
412: | Final production | Usually stops at generation | Requires separate tools | Requires separate tools | **Prompt → media → refinement → assembly** |
413: 
414: > **Others give users model access. Jiucaihezi Studio gives them a complete creative system.**
415: 
416: ---
417: 
418: ## 13. Product Direction
419: 
420: Jiucaihezi Studio starts with creative production because creative work exposes the limits of current AI systems most clearly:
421: 
422: - Long context
423: - Consistent characters and worlds
424: - Multi-stage production
425: - Reusable assets
426: - Human direction combined with AI execution
427: 
428: The same foundation can extend beyond filmmaking and media into many areas of work and daily life.
429: 
430: The long-term direction is to make advanced AI practical for people who do not want to learn model configuration, design software, or programming before they can benefit from it.
431: 
432: ---
433: 
434: ## 14. Our Vision
435: 
436: AI should not be reserved for people who understand code, models, or design.
437: 
438: Jiucaihezi Studio is being built for ordinary people with real work, real responsibilities, and real ideas.
439: 
440: Students, teachers, doctors, lawyers, delivery workers, small businesses, and independent creators should all be able to use powerful AI without mastering a complicated technical stack first.
441: 
442: Our goal is simple:
443: 
444: > **If you can speak, you can use the best AI.**
445: 
446: We are starting with creative work. We are building toward a system that helps people across work and everyday life.
447: 
448: **Not for a small group of experts.  
449: For the majority of people who are ready to make AI useful.**
450: 
451: ---
452: 
453: ## Product Access
454: 
455: **Website:** [https://jiucaihezi.studio/](https://jiucaihezi.studio/)  
456: **Company:** Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.

46: - 要国外邮箱，才能注册  
47: - 要身份验证  
48: - 要国外信用卡，才能付费  
49: - 还要时刻担心封号
50: 
51: **配置耗时巨大，门槛重重，风险不断。**
52: 
53: ---
54: 
55: ## 04｜卖点一：一个账号，中外模型全开
56: 
57: **一个账号登录。**  
58: 中外主流模型，统一调用。
59: 
60: - 不用来回换 App  
61: - 不用来回换账号  
62: - 不用为每个模型重配环境
63: 
64: **模型可以很多。入口，只需要一个。**
65: 
66: ---
67: 
68: ## 05｜模型矩阵：顶流都在
69: 
70: ### 大语言模型
71: 
72: - Claude：Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 5 / Fable 5  
73: - GPT：GPT 5.6（三模式全有）  
74: - Grok 4.5  
75: - Gemini 3.5 Flash  
76: - DeepSeek Pro  
77: - GLM 5.2
78: 
79: ### 生图 / 生视频
80: 
81: - CDA 12.0  
82: - Grok  
83: - Sora 2  
84: - GPT Image 2  
85: - Banana Pro  
86: - 以及更多国内、国外主流引擎
87: 
88: **中外顶流，一个账号点开就用。**
89: 
90: ---
91: 
92: ## 06｜痛点三：两条路，都废
93: 
94: 今天大家用 AI 创作，只剩两条路：
95: 
96: ### 路 A｜编程 Agent
97: 
98: - 浪费 Token  
99: - 也会忘  
100: - 全套设计偏编程，不贴创作
101: 
102: **又贵，又重，还记不住你的戏。**
103: 
104: ### 路 B｜直接聊大模型
105: 
106: - 对模型几乎没约束  
107: - 容易跑偏  
108: - 不能稳用 Skill / MCP / 插件  
109: - 能力砍一半，照样忘
110: 
111: **又飘，又弱，还是记不住。**
112: 
113: **两条路：一条笨且忘，一条飘且忘。**
114: 
115: ---
116: 
117: ## 07｜解法：创模式
118: 
119: 创模式，不是编程 Agent 换皮，也不是裸聊套壳。
120: 
121: 它同时拿走两边的优点：
122: 
123: - **像直连模型一样快**  
124: - **像编程 Agent 一样有约束、有工具**
125: 
126: 创模式能：
127: 
128: - 读写当前项目  
129: - 调用 Skill  
130: - 调用 MCP  
131: - 使用插件与工具  
132: - 直接驱动生图 / 生视频 / 生音频
133: 
134: **快，但不野。强，但不笨。**
135: 
136: ---
137: 
138: ## 08｜第二大脑·源头
139: 
140: 第二大脑的源头，来自 OpenAI 联合创始人 **Andrej Karpathy（卡帕西）** 的公开讨论与方案。
141: 
142: 核心观点一句话：
143: 
144: > **让模型像查维基百科一样，查询你的记忆。**
145: 
146: 文章与方案一经放出：
147: 
148: - GitHub 瞬间爆火  
149: - 星标暴涨  
150: - 引发全民讨论
151: 
152: **记忆不该赌上下文。记忆该像百科，可查、可更新、可复用。**
153: 
154: ---
155: 
156: ## 09｜第二大脑·五层 Skill
157: 
158: 我们把卡帕西的思路，加上 Obsidian 知识库方法，做成了：
159: 
160: **AI 维基百科式项目记忆。**
161: 
162: 后端是独创 **五层 Skill，五位一体**：
163: 
164: 1. **Everything 转 Wiki** —— 任何内容，先沉淀进库  
165: 2. **Raw 转 Wiki** —— 原始事实，压缩成可复用知识  
166: 3. **查询** —— 像查百科一样取回记忆  
167: 4. **一致性巡检** —— 查穿帮、漏改、断链、冲突  
168: 5. **修正** —— 发现问题，直接改回真相源
169: 
170: 达到的效果：
171: 
172: > **就算你写到 100 万字，**  
173: > **AI 也能知道第 100 章里，一个龙套男人手里拿的是什么武器。**
174: 
175: **不笨拙。不健忘。细节真的留得住。**
176: 
177: ---
178: 
179: ## 10｜流水线总览：四步成片
180: 
181: AI 做影视，不该在十几个软件里跳。
182: 
183: 韭菜盒子创作流水线，四步：
184: 
185: 1. **创模式 + Skill** → 生成提示词  
186: 2. **创作面板** → 生成图片 / 视频 / 音频  
187: 3. **画布** → 精修  
188: 4. **创模式 + 工具** → 拼装完整影片
189: 
190: **提示词、生成、精修、成片，一条链路走完。**
191: 
192: ---
193: 
194: ## 11｜第一步：创模式 + Skill，生成提示词
195: 
196: 文本侧工作，创模式 + Skill 最强。
197: 
198: 内置一整套精细 Skill，帮你把影片拆成可生成的提示词：
199: 
200: - 定风格  
201: - 写角色提示词  
202: - 写场景提示词  
203: - 写道具提示词  
204: - 写分镜提示词
205: 
206: 不是泛泛聊天写两句。  
207: 是按影视生产逻辑，把该写的提示词一次写全、写准。
208: 
209: **先把“怎么拍”写清楚，后面才生成得稳。**
210: 
211: ---
212: 
213: ## 12｜第二步：创作面板，生成资料
214: 
215: 提示词写好，直接进创作面板。
216: 
217: 在同一套系统里：
218: 
219: - 选中外模型  
220: - 生成图片  
221: - 生成视频  
222: - 生成音频
223: 
224: 不用再复制提示词去另一个 App。  
225: 不用再换账号、换界面、换历史记录。
226: 
227: **左边想清楚，右边直接出料。**
228: 
229: ---
230: 
231: ## 13｜第三步：画布精修
232: 
233: 生成出来，不等于能直接用。
234: 
235: 画布负责精修：
236: 
237: - 对细节图片做 AI P 图  
238: - 调、改、补、对齐  
239: - 把能用的留下，把不对的修准
240: 
241: 媒体不是下完就散。  
242: 继续留在项目里，随时复用。
243: 
244: **生成是起点，精修才进生产。**
245: 
246: ---
247: 
248: ## 14｜第四步：创模式 + 工具，拼装成片
249: 
250: 精修好的图片、视频、音频，回到创模式。
251: 
252: 用工具链继续往后走，例如：
253: 
254: - FFmpeg 等拼装能力  
255: - 把素材接成完整影片  
256: - 在同一项目里完成最后一公里
257: 
258: 不是“素材生成器”。  
259: 是能把结果真正拼起来的创作系统。
260: 
261: **从提示词，到素材，到精修，到成片。**  
262: **四步闭环。**
263: 
264: ---
265: 
266: ## 15｜下一步：贴近小白的表单式
267: 
268: 核心能力已经有了：创模式 + 创作面板 + 画布 + 工具成片。
269: 
270: 下一步，是把复杂能力做成小白也能直接填的表单：
271: 
272: - **电商模式**：已有，直观填写即可生成  
273: - **漫剧 / 小说 / 电影 / 电视剧**：后续按场景上线
274: 
275: 用户不必先懂 Skill 原理。  
276: 选场景，填表单，直接出结果。
277: 
278: **专业能力在底层。小白入口在表层。**
279: 
280: ---
281: 
282: ## 16｜对照表
283: 
284: |  | 各家官方产品 | 编程 Agent | 裸聊大模型 | **韭菜盒子** |
285: | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
286: | 模型入口 | 一家一个 | 偏开发 | 一家一个 | **一个账号全开** |
287: | 大陆上手 | 难 | 更难 | 难 | **开箱即用** |
288: | 创作约束 | 弱 | 强但偏编程 | 几乎无 | **创模式有约束** |
289: | Skill / MCP | 难统一 | 有，偏代码 | 弱 / 无 | **创作向可用** |
290: | 项目记忆 | 基本无 | 会忘且偏工程 | 最易忘 | **第二大脑五层** |
291: | 影视流水线 | 断裂 | 无 | 无 | **四步成片** |
292: 
293: **别人给入口。我们给完整创作系统。**
294: 
295: ---
296: 
297: ## 17｜你用韭菜盒子，会收获什么
298: 
299: 1. **一个账号，中外模型随便切**  
300: 2. **大陆也能低门槛用上国外模型**  
301: 3. **创模式：又快，又有工具和约束**  
302: 4. **第二大脑：百万字项目也记得住细节**  
303: 5. **四步流水线：提示词 → 媒体 → 精修 → 成片**  
304: 6. **APP + Web 双端，打开就能干**  
305: 7. **未来还有表单式场景，小白也能上手**
306: 
307: **少切换。少失忆。少断链。多出片。**
308: 
309: ---
310: 
311: ## 18｜愿景
312: 
313: AI 不该只属于懂编程、懂模型、懂设计的人。
314: 
315: 韭菜盒子想做的事很简单：
316: 
317: **让普通人，只要会说话，就能用上最好的 AI。**
318: 
319: 今天，我们从创作开始；
320: 未来，服务每个人的工作与生活。
321: 
322: **不是为少数专家打造，
323: 而是让绝大多数人都能真正用上 AI。**
324: 
325: Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.

12: 
13: # Jiucaihezi Studio
14: ## Product Overview
15: 
16: **Shanghai Chunqian Culture Media Co., Ltd.**  
17: **Web + Desktop App**  
18: **Website:** [jiucaihezi.studio](https://jiucaihezi.studio/)
19: 
20: ---
21: 
22: ## 1. What Is Jiucaihezi Studio?
23: 
24: Jiucaihezi Studio is an AI-powered creative workspace designed to help people move from ideas to finished creative work.
25: 
26: It brings models, creative workflows, project memory, media generation, editing, and production tools into one system.
27: 
28: The product is built around two core promises:
29: 
30: > **One account. Every model.**  
31: > **A Second Brain that never forgets.**
32: 
33: This is not simply another chatbot, image generator, or coding agent. It is a **Creative Agent** designed for continuous, structured creative production.
34: 
35: Users can work with leading models from China and around the world without constantly switching between separate products, accounts, interfaces, and histories.
36: 
37: ---
38: 
39: ## 2. Why Existing AI Workflows Break Down
40: 
41: AI has become part of everyday creative work, but the current experience is still fragmented.
42: 
43: ### One model usually means one product
44: 
45: Want to use Grok? Open Grok.  
46: Want GPT? Go to an OpenAI product.  
47: Want Claude? Switch to another platform.  
48: Want a China-based model? Open another app, website, or account.
49: 
50: Every switch can break the workflow:
51: 
52: - Different accounts
53: - Different interfaces
54: - Different billing systems
55: - Different histories
56: - Disconnected files and assets
57: - No shared project memory
58: 
59: The models keep getting better, but the creative process keeps getting split apart.
60: 
61: ### Accessing overseas models can also be difficult
62: 
63: For users in mainland China, accessing overseas AI products may require:
64: 
65: - A VPN or other network setup
66: - An overseas email address
67: - Identity verification
68: - An international payment method
69: - Ongoing concern about account restrictions or suspension
70: 
71: The setup is time-consuming, uncertain, and difficult for ordinary users.
72: 
73: ### The two common AI paths both have weaknesses
74: 
75: #### Coding Agents
76: 
77: Coding agents are powerful, but they are built around software repositories and programming workflows.
78: 
79: They often:
80: 
81: - Use a heavy read-write-confirmation process
82: - Consume large amounts of tokens
83: - Focus on engineering structure rather than creative structure
84: - Forget creative details even while preserving parts of the code workflow
85: 
86: They are powerful, but heavy, expensive, programming-oriented, and still forgetful.
87: 
88: #### Direct Model Chat
89: 
90: Directly chatting with a model is fast and accessible, but it often lacks the structure needed for a long creative project.
91: 
92: It can:
93: 
94: - Drift away from the intended direction
95: - Lose character, world-building, and continuity details
96: - Provide weak support for Skills, MCP, and plugins
97: - Leave the user responsible for manually organizing the project memory
98: 
99: It is fast, but loose, limited, and still forgetful.
100: 
101: > **The current choice is often between a heavy system that forgets and a lightweight system that also forgets.**
102: 
103: Jiucaihezi Studio is designed to remove that tradeoff.
104: 
105: ---
106: 
107: ## 3. One Account, Leading Models in One Workspace
108: 
109: Jiucaihezi Studio provides one unified entry point for models from China and around the world.
110: 
111: Users can choose the model that best fits the current task without leaving the project or rebuilding the workflow from scratch.
112: 
113: ### Large Language Models
114: 
115: The platform provides access to leading models, including:
116: 
117: - Claude Opus 4.8
118: - Claude Sonnet 5
119: - Claude Fable 5
120: - GPT 5.6, including its three modes
121: - Grok 4.5
122: - Gemini 3.5 Flash
123: - DeepSeek Pro
124: - GLM 5.2
125: - Other leading models from China and around the world
126: 
127: ### Image and Video Models
128: 
129: The platform also brings together image and video generation models, including:
130: 
131: - CDA 12.0
132: - Grok
133: - Sora 2
134: - GPT Image 2
135: - Banana Pro
136: - Other leading image and video models from China and around the world
137: 
138: The point is not simply to collect model names. The point is to make model choice part of one continuous creative workflow.
139: 
140: > **There can be many models. There only needs to be one creative entry point.**
141: 
142: ---
143: 
144: ## 4. Creative Mode: Fast Like Chat, Structured Like an Agent
145: 
146: Creative Mode is the core operating mode of Jiucaihezi Studio.
147: 
148: It is not a coding agent with a different visual interface. It is also not a basic chat wrapper.
149: 
150: Creative Mode combines the speed of direct model access with the structure, permissions, and tool capabilities of an agent.
151: 
152: It can:
153: 
154: - Read and write to the current project
155: - Use built-in Skills
156: - Call MCP tools
157: - Use plugins and other production tools
158: - Organize creative materials inside the project
159: - Generate images, video, and audio through the connected workflow
160: 
161: This makes the system both responsive and controlled:
162: 
163: - The model can respond directly instead of following a heavy programming path
164: - The project can still be read, updated, and organized through defined tools
165: - Creative Skills can guide the work instead of leaving every decision to an unconstrained conversation
166: 
167: > **Fast, but not uncontrolled.  Powerful, but not clumsy.**
168: 
169: ---
170: 
171: ## 5. The Second Brain: Memory That Can Be Looked Up
172: 
173: The idea behind the Second Brain is simple:
174: 
175: > **Let the model look up project memory the way it looks up Wikipedia.**
176: 
177: This concept was strongly discussed in the AI community through the work and public writing of **Andrej Karpathy**, an OpenAI co-founder. The idea quickly attracted widespread attention, rapid GitHub growth, and broad discussion about how AI should manage long-term memory.
178: 
179: The key insight is that memory should not depend only on a model's temporary context window.
180: 
181: Project memory should be:
182: 
183: - Searchable
184: - Structured
185: - Updateable
186: - Reusable
187: - Traceable
188: 
189: Jiucaihezi Studio combines this idea with the knowledge-base approach popularized by tools such as Obsidian to build an AI-Wikipedia-style memory system for creative projects.
190: 
191: The goal is straightforward: the AI should not merely sound as if it remembers. It should be able to retrieve the project's actual source of truth.
192: 
193: ---
194: 
195: ## 6. Five Integrated Skills Behind the Second Brain
196: 
197: The Second Brain is supported by five integrated Skills that work together as one memory system.
198: 
199: ### 1. Everything to Wiki
200: 
201: Turn conversations, documents, scripts, notes, and other material into structured project knowledge.
202: 
203: ### 2. Raw to Wiki
204: 
205: Trace confirmed knowledge back to source facts and preserve the original evidence behind the project memory.
206: 
207: ### 3. Query
208: 
209: Search and retrieve project memory as if consulting an encyclopedia.
210: 
211: ### 4. Consistency Check
212: 
213: Detect continuity errors, missed changes, broken links, naming drift, and conflicts across the project.
214: 
215: ### 5. Correction
216: 
217: Apply verified corrections to the source of truth so the project can continue from a clean, consistent foundation.
218: 
219: Together, these five Skills create a complete memory loop:
220: 
221: > **Capture. Preserve. Retrieve. Check. Correct.**
222: 
223: The result is designed for long-form work. Even in a one-million-word project, the AI can still answer a detail such as:
224: 
225: > What weapon was a minor character carrying in Chapter 100?
226: 
227: That is the difference between a model that merely remembers a conversation and a system that maintains project knowledge.
228: 
229: ---
230: 
231: ## 7. From Story to Finished Film
232: 
233: Jiucaihezi Studio is designed to connect the major stages of AI-assisted creative production.
234: 
235: A long-form project can move through a connected path:
236: 
237: ```text
238: Idea / IP
239:   → Novel and long-form story development
240:   → Script and episode development
241:   → Characters, scenes, props, and storyboards
242:   → Image generation
243:   → Video and audio generation
244:   → Canvas refinement
245:   → Final assembly and production
246: ```
247: 
248: The current core workflow is organized into four connected steps.
249: 
250: ### Step 1 — Creative Mode + Skills
251: 
252: Use Creative Mode and built-in Skills to develop the text and production instructions:
253: 
254: - Lock the visual style
255: - Build character prompts
256: - Build scene prompts
257: - Build prop prompts
258: - Build storyboard and shot prompts
259: - Convert creative decisions into precise, usable generation prompts
260: 
261: ### Step 2 — Creation Panel
262: 
263: Move the prompts into the Creation Panel and select the appropriate model from the unified model system.
264: 
265: Generate:
266: 
267: - Images
268: - Video
269: - Audio
270: 
271: The prompt does not need to be copied into another product. The model does not require a separate account or a separate production history.
272: 
273: ### Step 3 — Canvas Refinement
274: 
275: Use the Canvas to refine the generated material:
276: 
277: - AI image editing
278: - Detail repair
279: - Local changes
280: - Extension and adjustment
281: - Visual alignment
282: 
283: Generation is the starting point. Refinement turns a generated result into usable production material.
284: 
285: ### Step 4 — Creative Mode + Tools
286: 
287: Bring the refined assets back into the project and use Creative Mode with production tools to complete the work.
288: 
289: The toolchain can support tasks such as:
290: 
291: - Combining image, video, and audio assets
292: - Adding subtitles and other production elements
293: - Using tools such as FFmpeg to assemble clips
294: - Building a complete video or film sequence
295: 
296: > **Prompt. Generate. Refine. Assemble.**  
297: > **One connected creative production loop.**
298: 
299: ---
300: 
301: ## 8. Easier Entry Points for Beginners
302: 
303: The core system is built for professional creative work, but not every user should need to understand the underlying Skills.
304: 
305: Jiucaihezi Studio is also developing simple, scenario-based form workflows.
306: 
307: The first example is **E-commerce Mode**, which is already available. Users can fill out a guided form and generate product content without understanding the deeper Skill architecture.
308: 
309: Additional scenario-based workflows are planned for:
310: 
311: - Manhua and short-form drama
312: - Novel writing
313: - Film
314: - Television
315: - Other creative and business scenarios
316: 
317: The product direction is simple:
318: 
319: > **Professional power underneath. Simple entry points on top.**
320: 
321: ---
322: 
323: ## 9. Web and Desktop App
324: 
325: Jiucaihezi Studio is available through two entry points.
326: 
327: ### Web Version
328: 
329: Visit [https://jiucaihezi.studio/](https://jiucaihezi.studio/) to start using the product in a browser.
330: 
331: ### Desktop App
332: 
333: The desktop app provides a dedicated environment for deeper project work and can be downloaded from the product interface.
334: 
335: Both versions are built around the same product system, model access, project structure, and creative workflow.
336: 
337: ---
338: 
339: ## 10. How to Get Started
340: 
341: ### Step 1 — Open the product
342: 
343: Go to:
344: 
345: [https://jiucaihezi.studio/](https://jiucaihezi.studio/)
346: 
347: You can begin through the Web version.
348: 
349: ### Step 2 — Register and sign in
350: 
351: Open **Settings** to create an account or sign in.
352: 
353: ### Step 3 — Choose a model
354: 
355: After signing in, use the model selector in the upper-right corner of the conversation panel.
356: 
357: Choose the model that best fits the task and start working.
358: 
359: ### Step 4 — Choose a mode
360: 
361: Jiucaihezi Studio currently provides three main modes:
362: 
363: - **Chat Mode** — discuss ideas and ask questions without directly operating the computer

3: # 韭菜盒子 Studio
4: 
5: ## 创作 Agent 产品介绍
6: 
7: **封面金句**  
8: 创作的人，终于不用在「浪费」和「失忆」之间二选一。  
9: 也终于不用为每一个模型，再下载一套产品。
10: 
11: ---
12: 
13: ## 01｜先把今天的创作现实说清楚
14: 
15: 现在几乎所有内容团队都在用 AI。  
16: 问题不在「用没用」，而在**怎么用**。
17: 
18: 现实里，创作者同时卡在两道死结上。
19: 
20: ### 死结一：能力路径二选一，都很疼
21: 
22: **路 A：用编程 Agent 硬扛创作**  
23: Codex、Claude Code 一类工具，能力很强。  
24: 但它们天生为代码仓库设计：
25: 
26: - 为了「稳」，大量读写、反复确认、上下文堆叠  
27: - Token 烧得快  
28: - 换到剧本、角色、分镜、世界观时，路径笨拙  
29: - 细节记得住工程结构，却很难像主创一样记住「这部戏的人设真相」
30: 
31: **强，但贵；能跑，但不像创作者。**
32: 
33: **路 B：直接跟大模型聊**  
34: GPT、Claude Opus、Gemini、DeepSeek……打开就写，确实省。  
35: 但省下来的代价是：
36: 
37: - 聊完就淡  
38: - 角色前后漂移  
39: - 设定散落在聊天记录里  
40: - 上一集定过的东西，下一集要重新喂  
41: - 项目越长，越像在跟一个聪明但健忘的助理反复对齐
42: 
43: **快，但飘；省，但失忆。**
44: 
45: > **要么笨拙地贵，要么轻巧地忘。**
46: 
47: ### 死结二：模型越强，工具越碎
48: 
49: 今天想用 Grok，得进 Grok 的产品。  
50: 想用 GPT，得进 OpenAI 体系：ChatGPT、Codex……  
51: 想用 Claude，得进 Anthropic 那一套。  
52: 想用国内模型，再换另一批 App、网页、账号、额度、工作流。
53: 
54: 结果是：
55: 
56: - 账号一堆  
57: - 界面一堆  
58: - 历史一堆  
59: - 资产一堆  
60: - 同一个角色，在 A 工具画完，在 B 工具接着写，在 C 工具再生成视频——**上下文全断**
61: 
62: 模型没有变少，创作却被切碎了。
63: 
64: 所以今天真正的痛点不是「AI 不够聪明」。  
65: 是：
66: 
67: > **能力分散在无数产品里；  
68: > 记忆消散在无数对话框里；  
69: > 生产线断裂在无数切换里。**
70: 
71: ---
72: 
73: ## 02｜韭菜盒子解决的，就是这组死结
74: 
75: **韭菜盒子 Studio**  
76: 面向内容创作的 AI 工作台。  
77: **桌面 APP（macOS / Windows）+ Web 端**。
78: 
79: 它不是又一个聊天框，也不是把编程 Agent 换皮成创作界面。  
80: 它同时做三件关键的事：
81: 
82: 1. **一个账号，打通中外主流模型** —— 不再为每个模型单独换产品  
83: 2. **第二大脑，记住项目细节** —— 不再在「省 token」和「能记住」之间妥协  
84: 3. **完整流水线，从小说到影像** —— 不再把写作、设定、生图、生视频拆成孤岛
85: 
86: 一句话：
87: 
88: > **用户主导，AI 执行。  
89: > 模型负责聪明，系统负责统一入口、长期记忆、全链路落地。**
90: 
91: ---
92: 
93: ## 03｜卖点一：一个账号，中外模型全开
94: 
95: 这是最直观、也最能立刻被感受到的能力。
96: 
97: ### 以前怎么用模型
98: 
99: | 你想用的能力 | 你通常要做的事 |
100: | --- | --- |
101: | Grok | 下载 / 打开 Grok 相关产品 |
102: | GPT | 进入 OpenAI 产品体系（ChatGPT、Codex 等） |
103: | Claude / Opus | 进入对应官方产品 |
104: | Gemini | 再换一套 Google 侧产品 |
105: | 国内模型 | 再开一批国内 App / 网页 / 账号 |
106: 
107: 每一次切换，都不只是换一个 logo。  
108: 是在换：
109: 
110: - 账号体系  
111: - 交互习惯  
112: - 历史记录  
113: - 文件位置  
114: - 项目上下文
115: 
116: ### 韭菜盒子怎么做
117: 
118: **一个账号登录，即可调用中外主流模型。**  
119: 写、画、拍、改，都在同一工作台完成。  
120: 不用为了换模型，再换一整套产品。
121: 
122: 对创作者意味着：
123: 
124: - **选型自由**：哪个模型适合剧本就用哪个，适合生图就用哪个  
125: - **路径统一**：同一项目里切换模型，不离开工作流  
126: - **资产不断**：结果回到同一项目，而不是散落在十个 App  
127: - **团队友好**：不要求每个人装齐全网工具，只要求进入一个系统
128: 
129: > **模型可以很多。  
130: > 创作入口，只需要一个。**
131: 
132: ---
133: 
134: ## 04｜核心引擎：创模式
135: 
136: 创模式是韭菜盒子真正不同于「普通模型聊天」和「编程 Agent」的地方。
137: 
138: 它不是套壳问答，而是一条创作运行时：
139: 
140: 1. **直连大模型决策** —— 用最强模型的判断力，不绕远路  
141: 2. **按需加载专业 Skill** —— 编剧、小说、角色、场景、分镜、媒体提示词……需要哪个上哪个  
142: 3. **读写当前项目** —— 结果落进文件，不是飘在对话框  
143: 4. **生成图片 / 视频 / 音频** —— 媒体任务进同一项目链路  
144: 5. **自动沉淀事实** —— 说过什么、改过什么、生成过什么，可追溯
145: 
146: 所以它既不像编程 Agent 那样为了「工程闭环」把流程做笨；  
147: 也不像裸聊模型那样写完就散。
148: 
149: > **创模式 = 聪明的执行层 + 专业的能力包 + 可沉淀的项目手脚。**
150: 
151: ---
152: 
153: ## 05｜真正的破局点：第二大脑
154: 
155: 第二大脑，是韭菜盒子的命门能力。  
156: 不是 slogan，是工程。
157: 
158: ### 别人为什么会忘？
159: 
160: - 聊天 AI：上下文有限，项目真相不在文件里  
161: - 编程 Agent：记得住仓库，不天然理解「角色圣经 / 场景资产 / 剧情连续性」  
162: - 多产品切换：上下文在工具之间彻底断裂  
163: - 网盘 + 微信 + 文档：人记得住入口，系统接不上
164: 
165: ### 韭菜盒子怎么记？
166: 
167: | 层 | 做什么 | 价值 |
168: | --- | --- | --- |
169: | **项目 Wiki** | 角色 / 场景 / 道具 / 世界 / 剧情结构化沉淀 | 项目有单一真相源 |
170: | **热记忆 hot** | 当前最关键事实优先注入模型 | 每次开聊都先对齐主线 |
171: | **事实账本 .raw** | 用户意图、AI 动作、文件变更可追溯 | 不是“好像说过”，而是有据可查 |
172: | **巡检与修正** | 查穿帮、漏改、断链、设定冲突 | 长项目不靠人肉回忆 |
173: | **对话转 Wiki** | 把过程沉淀成可复用知识 | 聊过的不会白聊 |
174: 
175: 结果是：
176: 
177: > **不笨拙：不必用编程 Agent 的重路径硬扛创作；  
178: > 不健忘：关键细节写进项目，而不是赌模型上下文；  
179: > 不碎片：模型切换发生在系统内，记忆仍在项目里。**
180: 
181: 对影视与内容团队，这意味着：  
182: 一部戏最贵的不是生成一张图，  
183: 是**角色不飘、世界观不散、多集不断、资产可复用。**
184: 
185: ---
186: 
187: ## 06｜完整流水线：从文字到影像
188: 
189: 大多数 AI 工具停在单点：  
190: 会写，或会画，或会动一下。
191: 
192: 韭菜盒子复现的是内容工业真正需要的链路：
193: 
194: ```
195: 灵感 / IP
196:    ↓
197: 小说（长线叙事）
198:    ↓
199: 剧本（分集、成稿、爽点、钩子）
200:    ↓
201: 工程化素材（角色、场景、道具、分镜）
202:    ↓
203: 生图（设定图、空镜、海报、物料）
204:    ↓
205: 生视频（镜头、动态、成片素材）
206:    ↓
207: 第二大脑持续记忆与巡检
208: ```
209: 
210: **不是五个散装功能。**  
211: 是一条能从头跑到尾、并且中途不丢记忆的生产线。  
212: 再叠加「一个账号调用中外模型」，整条链路终于不用在十几个产品之间跳。
213: 
214: ---
215: 
216: ## 07｜五个必须被看见的能力
217: 
218: ### ① 写小说：长线叙事可续写
219: 
220: - 灵感到成稿全流程  
221: - 核心梗、角色网、大纲、章纲、正文、复盘  
222: - 进入 Wiki，可续写、可巡检、可修正
223: 
224: 小说不是一次聊完就散。  
225: 它变成**可生长的 IP 资产。**
226: 
227: ---
228: 
229: ### ② 写剧本：从梗概到可拍摄成稿
230: 
231: - 短剧 / 漫剧 / 爽剧专业流  
232: - 世界观 + 极致角色 + 催化剂  
233: - 分集大纲 → 逐集成稿  
234: - 反转、卡点、钩子、临摹换皮 / 从零涌现
235: 
236: 不是「帮你写个剧情」。  
237: 是按内容生产逻辑组织的**剧本生产线。**
238: 
239: ---
240: 
241: ### ③ 生图：出的是资产，不是灵感截图
242: 
243: - 文生图 / 图生图  
244: - 多模型可选（同一账号内切换）  
245: - 角色设定、场景空镜、道具多视图、海报物料  
246: - 结果回写项目，可进画布继续用
247: 
248: 对团队真正有价值的是：  
249: **可复用、可对齐、可进入下一环节。**
250: 
251: ---
252: 
253: ### ④ 生视频：嵌在项目流里，不是孤立按钮
254: 
255: - 多引擎视频生成  
256: - 图生视频 / 镜头驱动  
257: - 与分镜、角色、场景提示词衔接
258: 
259: 重点不是「能动一下」，  
260: 而是镜头结果仍然活在同一项目记忆里，  
261: 后面还能继续改、继续接、继续生产。
262: 
263: ---
264: 
265: ### ⑤ 第二大脑：让整条流水线不崩
266: 
267: 没有第二大脑，上面四项都会在第二周开始变形。  
268: 有了第二大脑：
269: 
270: - 角色脸和性格不漂  
271: - 场景规则不乱  
272: - 前后集不断档  
273: - 团队换人也能接上
274: 
275: > **第二大脑不是附加功能。  
276: > 它是整条创作流水线的稳定器。**
277: 
278: ---
279: 
280: ## 08｜APP + Web：同一套创作系统
281: 
282: | 端 | 形态 | 价值 |
283: | --- | --- | --- |
284: | **桌面 APP** | macOS / Windows | 全能力工作台：创模式、项目文件、Skill、媒体生成、画布、深度创作 |
285: | **Web 端** | 浏览器即开 | 轻量进入创模式，降低启动成本，随时继续创作 |
286: 
287: 双端共享同一产品哲学：
288: 
289: - 同一个项目思维  
290: - 同一套 Skill 能力  
291: - 同一条媒体任务链路  
292: - 同一个账号下的模型能力  
293: - 同一种「用户主导、AI 执行」
294: 
295: **不是两个产品，是一个创作系统的两种入口。**
296: 
297: ---
298: 
299: ## 09｜为什么这不是套壳
300: 
301: | 维度 | 裸聊大模型 | 编程 Agent | 各家官方单点产品 | **韭菜盒子 Studio** |
302: | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
303: | 创作门槛 | 低 | 高 | 中 | **低（说人话）** |
304: | Token / 路径成本 | 相对省 | 常偏贵、偏重 | 单点计费 | **创作路径更直接** |
305: | 项目记忆 | 差 | 偏工程记忆 | 基本不跨产品记 | **Wiki + hot + 事实账本** |
306: | 模型入口 | 一家一个 | 偏开发场景 | 一家一个 App/网页 | **一个账号，中外模型统一调用** |
307: | 小说 / 剧本专业流 | 泛写 | 无 | 弱 / 无 | **专业 Skill 生产线** |
308: | 图视频是否回项目 | 通常否 | 无 | 下载即断 | **资产回写 + 画布** |
309: | 全链路闭环 | 无 | 无 | 无 | **小说→剧本→制作完整流水线** |
310: 
311: 最关键的三句：
312: 
313: > **别人给你的是模型入口。**  
314: > **韭菜盒子给你的是统一入口 + 长期记忆 + 创作操作系统。**  
315: > **模型再多，也不该逼创作者活在切换里。**

320: 
321: 1. **不再在「贵且笨」和「省且忘」之间妥协**
322: 
323: 既用得上强模型，又留得住项目细节。
324: 
325: 1. **不再为每个模型单独养一套工具**
326: 
327: 一个账号覆盖中外主流模型，选型在系统内完成。
328: 
329: 1. **从灵感工具升级为生产能力**

331: 小说、剧本、设定、画面、视频在同一系统内推进。

333: 1. **长项目第一次真正适合 AI**

337: 1. **资产可复用，而不是聊天记录可翻找**

346: **统一入口，才配谈效率。**

351: - **一个账号** —— 中外模型统一调用  
352: - **创模式** —— 负责执行

45: ## 📖 Wiki 使用说明
46: 
47: Wiki 是项目的第二大脑。你扔内容进来，AI 整理成能搜索、能追踪的知识库。
48: 
49: ### 五个 Skill
50: 
51: | Skill              | 干啥                                           | 指令                           |
52: | ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
53: | jc-everything-wiki | 按内容标准建立或接管 Wiki 结构                 | `建立项目 Wiki`                |
54: | jc-raw-wiki        | 填充已确认事实、刷新记忆                       | `填充 Wiki`                    |
55: | jc-cha-wiki        | 查状态、搜内容、画关系图                       | `查询[关键词]` 或 `生成关系图` |
56: | jc-jian-wiki       | 检查断链、矛盾和结构风险，只查不改             | `巡检一下`                     |
57: | jc-xiu-wiki        | 把确定性巡检问题落地修正；给 Wiki 加新分类目录 | `执行修正` 或 `加个分类`       |
58: 
59: 巡检官只查不改，修正官接手把体检单上的问题落地——机械类（漏改/断链）直接改，语义类（设定矛盾）先问你哪个对。"巡检→修正→复巡" 是闭环，不然体检单会一直堆着没人处理。
60: `jc-xiu-wiki` 还管架构扩展：项目做大了想给 Wiki 加新分类（比如小说中途想单独建“伏笔/”目录），它只建骨架；需要理解旧内容并迁移时交给 `jc-raw-wiki`。
61: 
62: ### 怎么用
63: 
64: ```
65: 建立项目 Wiki                     → 建库或接管
66: 填充 Wiki                         → 填入已确认内容
67: 查看 Wiki 状态                    → 看进度
68: 查询[关键词]                       → 搜东西
69: 生成关系图                         → 画关系网（需 Obsidian Canvas Viewer 插件）
70: 巡检一下                           → 体检，出体检单
71: 执行修正                           → 把体检单上的问题落地改掉
72: 加个分类                           → 给 wiki 建新的子目录/分类
73: ```
74: 
75: ### Wiki 目录约定
76: 
77: | 目录     | 放什么                         |
78: | -------- | ------------------------------ |
79: | `架构/`  | 产品架构、对照表、翻译方针     |
80: | `开发/`  | SDD、审计、历史、排障          |
81: | `运维/`  | 服务器、模型注册、模型矩阵     |
82: | `排障/`  | RH 图片、Intel Mac 兼容        |
83: | `学习/`  | AI 编程生存手册                |
84: | `归档/`  | 历史 SDD、handover、模型文档   |
85: | `hot.md` | 热缓存（当前最需要被读的文档） |
86: | `log.md` | 操作日志（append-only）        |

90: ## 🔧 知识库维护
91: 
92: | 什么时候          | 做什么                                      |
93: | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
94: | 每次收尾          | 刷新 `hot.md`（更新日期、指向最新变更）     |
95: | 踩了新类型坑      | 加一条到生存手册（#N + 速查表）             |
96: | 分支合并/发版     | 更新 `开发历史.md`                          |
97: | 功能或排障完成    | 用 `jc-raw-wiki`（记忆压缩）归档经验到 wiki |
98: | 不确定知识库状态  | 用 `jc-cha-wiki` 查状态、生成关系图         |
99: | 修了 bug/发现问题 | 更新 `OpenCode差异修复记录.md`              |
100: | 架构变化          | 更新 `产品架构.md` + `对照表.md`            |
